features.name: Data Inspection & ID Mapping
features.description: Load DE stats and pseudobulk AnnData/MuData objects, extract obs/var tables, build Ensembl-HGNC-UniProt-Entrez identifier mapping with ambiguity reporting
features.evidence: Inspection DONE (examples/ inspectors). ID mapping DONE: src/tcell_pipeline/id_mapping.py extracts unique Ensembl from backed DE .obs[target_contrast] + .var[gene_ids] (never loads layers), maps Ensembl->HGNC offline via obs/var crossref and Ensembl->UniProt/Entrez via mygene.info. Ran online on real DE file: 12311 unique Ensembl (11526 targets, 10282 measured, 9497 both), all HGNC resolved, UniProt/Entrez filled. One-to-many UniProt DISAMBIGUATED (choose_uniprot): candidate accessions restricted to the gene's reviewed human canonical (UniProt REST gene_exact+reviewed), picked by annotation-score then lexical; uniprot_ambiguous marks ONLY equal-evidence ties (top score not strictly > runner-up). 33 multi-accession genes -> 23 resolved to a confident canonical (15 single reviewed e.g. CALM1/2/3 -> P0DP23/24/25, + 8 score-decisive where a fragment/secondary loses on annotation score), 10 genuine equal-evidence multi-product loci (CDKN2A p16/p14ARF, GNAS, MOCS2 bicistronic, TMPO isoforms) flagged uniprot_ambiguous with all candidates kept in uniprot_alternatives. Kept the gene as the perturbation unit (CRISPRi knocks down the whole locus); no forced single-protein pick for true ties. Wrote data/intermediate/id_mapping.parquet (adds uniprot_alternatives, uniprot_ambiguous) + ambiguity_report.txt. Delivered in the Module 0 pipeline (run_module0.py orchestrator). init.sh green (compileall + 20 pytest).

features.name: Leakage-Safe Train/Val/Test Splits
features.description: Construct splits that block gene families, protein complexes, and close graph neighborhoods from leaking between train and test; hash and freeze split files
features.evidence: src/tcell_pipeline/splits.py + docs/specs/2026-07-15-feat-003-leakage-safe-splits.md (design derived from the experiment-plan report: Training Splits, Phase-1 Split Lock, Experimental Units/Partitions). Empirical measurement on the real marts (11525 target genes) forced the algorithm: naive connected-components collapses on every axis (physical_ppi 1-hop -> 95% giant component, complex co-membership -> 23%, raw ESM cosine>=0.95 -> 92%, centered single-link -> 15%, Louvain -> 42%). Hard block = sequence/paralog family via REPRESENTATIVE (non-chaining, CD-HIT-style) clustering on CENTERED ESM-2 embeddings at cosine>=0.85 (measured largest family 3.1%), merged with direct CORUM complex co-membership under a 5%-of-genes size cap (capped union-find refuses giant merges: 3986 refused on real data). Physical-PPI neighbourhood is AUDIT-ONLY, not a partition constraint (per report G1 gate + Phase-1 step 5/6/9: 'cluster sequence/paralog similarity, separately stress PPI neighbourhoods, publish the similarity distribution'). 4-role partition per report (train/val/calibration/challenge ~60/15/10/15) assigned by whole family group, seeded, deficit-greedy; realized on real data 62.5/13.0/7.9/16.6%. Random diagnostic split (row-level) as the sanity baseline. Frozen + hashed to data/splits/ (git-tracked): blocked_target_ood.csv, random.csv, manifest.json (seed/thresholds/cap/sha256/realized fractions), leakage_report.json (machine-readable audit: hard-asserts no family group split across roles, publishes per-axis train->challenge residual). Effectiveness validated: blocking cuts the fraction of challenge genes with a >=0.85 train paralog from 53.5% (random) to 28.1% (blocked) = 47% reduction. config: SPLITS_ROOT/SPLIT_ROLES/SPLIT_FRACTIONS/SPLIT_SEED/SEQ_SIM_COSINE_THRESHOLD/GROUP_SIZE_CAP/SPLIT_AUDIT_HOPS + artifact paths. 8 synthetic tests (src/tests/test_splits.py): paralog+complex grouping, size-cap refusal, no family split across roles, seed determinism, singleton distribution, leakage-audit pass + fail-closed on injected split, random-split fractions, grouping ignores non-q_pre columns. init.sh green (compileall + 54 pytest). ponytail: centered-cosine threshold + cap are calibration knobs (report defers tuning to the published distribution); physical block is audit-only by data necessity; curated-family download and pairwise-must-link residual reduction are documented upgrade paths. UPDATE (2026-07-15, post code-review; split CSVs byte-identical, sha256 unchanged): three leakage-audit bugs fixed in splits.py -- (1) audit now publishes cap-induced family splits via an uncapped pre-cap component pass (the post-cap 'no split' assertion was blind to families the 5% cap must break: cap_induced_family_splits=1, family_challenge_sharing_train_frac=0.41, an upper bound inflated by single-linkage chaining); (2) sequence residual now centers train+challenge in one global frame (was per-subset means -> mismatched frames understating similarity); (3) run() fails closed when PLM embeddings are absent instead of silently publishing a sequence-leaky split as safe (override via SPLITS_ALLOW_NO_SEQUENCE). manifest gains sequence_block_active + n_genes_with_embedding. Corrected effectiveness: 53.8% (random) -> 26.4% (blocked) = 51% reduction (supersedes the 53.5/28.1/47 figures above). Tier-3: diagnostic random split (assign_random) now uses cumulative-boundary allocation (last boundary == n, no truncated tail) -- random.csv regenerated; blocked_target_ood.csv byte-identical to the 35e3999 freeze and effectiveness numbers unchanged (gene-level baseline doesn't shift at N=11525). Dead config SPLIT_AUDIT_HOPS removed. +1 regression test (random split covers all items at small N). See docs/reviews/2026-07-15-code-review-feat-016-feat-003.md.

features.name: Simple Baselines
features.description: Implement no-effect, condition-mean, ridge, elastic-net, nearest-neighbor, low-rank, and CatBoost baselines with common output schema
features.evidence: IN-PROGRESS (2026-07-16, Module 6 — Simple Baselines). New package src/tcell_pipeline/baselines/: simple_baselines.py with a common BaseBaseline protocol (fit(X,z,conditions)->predict(X,conditions)->(delta_z (M,K), delta_x (M,G)); gene space decoded via delta_x = delta_z @ B.T against the frozen fold-local basis B — the decoder's B @ delta_z pathway; basis=None -> empty (M,0) gene block so program-only eval stays usable). Six baselines: ZeroBaseline (no-effect), PerturbedMeanBaseline (Systema non-control/perturbed mean = avg train perturbation effect), ConditionMeanBaseline (per-condition mean, unseen condition -> global fallback), RidgeBaseline (multi-output Ridge on context features), NearestNeighborBaseline (kNN mean of neighbours' delta_z by target/context profile), LowRankBaseline (truncated-SVD program subspace + ridge map into it + reconstruct). Shared output store src/tcell_pipeline/evaluation/output_schema.py: predictions/<model>/<split>/<seed>.parquet (row_index + delta_z_0..K-1 + delta_x_0..G-1 + sigma_0..K-1; atomic via write_parquet_atomic; column read-back sorted by int suffix; baselines with no calibrated uncertainty write sigma=0). config: PREDICTIONS_ROOT. Verified: src/tests/test_baselines.py (11 test functions = 16 collected cases, the per-baseline fit+predict shape test parametrized across all 6 baselines: shapes [program + decoded gene], zero-returns-zeros, gene decode == dz@B.T, basis=None empty block, condition grouping + unseen-condition fallback, perturbed-mean == train average, ridge learns [err < 0.25x zero], low-rank learns + rank truncation shape, kNN k=1 returns neighbour values, single-program K==1 column-safe shapes); ./init.sh green at 131 tests, zero warnings. DEFERRED (out of the Module 6 goal's scope, remain for feat-006 done): elastic-net multitask regression + CatBoost/gradient-boosting baseline (both named in the feature description). Graph baselines (feat-007) + external comparators (feat-010) are explicitly out of scope here. POST-REVIEW (2026-07-16, adversarial workflow review — docs/reviews/2026-07-16-code-review-module6.md): RidgeBaseline/LowRankBaseline with K==1 (single program) or rank==1 had np.atleast_2d transpose sklearn's 1-D prediction to (1,M) -> matmul/decode shape error; FIXED with a column-safe _as_columns helper ((M,1) not (1,M)) + a single-program regression test. POST-REVIEW ROUND 2 (2026-07-16, xhigh workflow-backed /code-review): the shared fit/predict contract was inconsistent on absent inputs -- RidgeBaseline/NearestNeighborBaseline/LowRankBaseline crashed with opaque sklearn errors on X=None while zero/perturbed_mean/condition_mean accepted it, and ConditionMeanBaseline.predict raised on conditions=None. FIXED: a requires_features class flag routes X=None to a clear ValueError('<Baseline> requires a feature matrix X') for the three feature-based baselines while the feature-free baselines still accept X=None, and ConditionMeanBaseline.predict degrades to the global perturbed mean when conditions is None -- so a driver iterating BASELINES uniformly with predict(X) works. +5 regression tests (test_baselines.py now 14 functions / 23 collected cases). ./init.sh green at 145 tests. ELASTIC-NET + H1 TABULAR COMPARATOR BAR (2026-07-20). Landed the first of the two DEFERRED items: ElasticNetBaseline (BASELINES['elastic_net']) = StandardScaler -> MultiOutputRegressor(ElasticNet(alpha=0.1, l1_ratio=0.5, max_iter=2000, tol=1e-3), n_jobs=-1). Per-output (parallel across the K programs) NOT MultiTaskElasticNet: the coupled-L21 multitask descent is O(iters x features x samples x K) and ground for >17 min without converging on 1412 features x 128 programs, while the parallel per-output form fits the full train fold in 65 s. alpha/tol are set for CONVERGENCE, not the score -- more regularisation only WEAKENS the linear model, so it cannot flatter elastic-net against H1; the fit still ends at a near-tolerance duality gap (2.5e4 vs 1.3e4), which does not move systema materially. The scaler is fit inside the pipeline on train only (no val leakage). New run_module8_real.py --part baselines (run_tabular_baselines): fits each simple baseline on the REAL train fold predicting delta_z from the target gene's STATIC graph node feature (1412-d; 98.2% train / 97.9% val targets in-graph, zero-vector otherwise), scores the REAL val fold through the SAME response_metric_suite as feat-010, and ranks vs the frozen H1 via the shared summarize_vs_h1. Same leakage fence as feat-010 (train responses only; val features are frozen static node features; train_mean from train), and blocked-target OOD means val targets are DISJOINT from train (verified train-intersect-val = 0), so this is a genuine generalisation bar, not memorisation. These are NOT external comparators: no feat-010 comparator-family cap is consumed and they are not registered in the experiment registry. RESULT (full fold, 21,262/4,400): elastic_net +0.0342 > ridge +0.0206 > zero +0.0197 > low_rank +0.0169 > perturbed_mean +0.0123 > nearest_neighbor +0.0042; the frozen H1 condition_gated +0.0834 BEATS the strongest tabular baseline by +0.0492, well outside the 0.01 noise band. HONEST READ: a trained-predictor win, NOT graph value -- the no-graph expression_only (0.0861) beats the tabular bar too, exactly as in feat-010. Artifacts: data/results/comparators/tabular_baselines_{val.parquet,vs_h1.json}. STILL DEFERRED for feat-006 done: the CatBoost/gradient-boosting baseline (needs a new dependency; TabPFN likewise, plus a pretrained-weight download). +3 tests (test_baselines.py now 26 collected). ./init.sh green at 302. CORRECTION (2026-07-20, xhigh /code-review of f1a00dd). Baseline systema values are UNCHANGED (elastic_net +0.0342 still the strongest; H1 +0.0834; margin +0.0492) but three reporting defects are fixed. (1) summarize_vs_h1 was reused VERBATIM, so tabular_baselines_vs_h1.json labelled every baseline kind='comparator' and carried the external-comparator basis string -- contradicting this feature's own 'NOT external comparators, no cap consumed' claim and mis-counting them for any later feat-010 comparator-family-cap audit that scans these JSONs. kind/basis are now parameters; the artifact reports kind='baseline' with a baseline-specific basis. (2) Off-graph targets get an all-zero feature row indistinguishable from real data, and the coverage counts were printed to stdout ONLY. Now PERSISTED as feature_coverage: 20,877/21,262 train and 4,309/4,400 val targets are in-graph, so 385 train and 91 val rows carry a degenerate all-zero feature vector and receive one constant prediction -- which depresses the feature-regressing baselines and INFLATES the H1 margin. (3) ElasticNetBaseline.fit_diagnostics() now records n_iter_max/max_iter/converged/nonzero_coef_frac, and it shows the bar is UNDER-FIT: n_iter_max=2000 == max_iter, converged=False, nonzero_coef_frac=0.064. The published +0.0492 is therefore an UPPER BOUND on H1's advantage -- a converged bar could only score higher and shrink the margin. The original docstring argued only that more regularisation weakens the model, which is the safe direction for a competitor but the WRONG direction for a floor H1 must clear. Still deferred for feat-006 done: CatBoost/gradient-boosting and TabPFN (new dependency + weight download), and a converged elastic-net bar. +2 tests. ./init.sh green at 314.

features.name: Graph Baselines
features.description: Implement network propagation, untyped GNN, and typed static graph baselines using the PPI graph from feat-004
features.evidence: DONE (2026-07-16, Module 7 — Graph Baselines; walkthrough §10.6, report §Baselines). src/tcell_pipeline/baselines/graph_baselines.py: three PPI-graph references, each isolating one variable. (1) NetworkPropagationBaseline — non-neural symmetric-normalised diffusion (What=D^-1/2 A D^-1/2; F<-r*S0+(1-r)*What@F, n_iter power iterations); fit places each training target's mean dz + presence on its protein node, predict = F_signal/F_presence (graph-proximity-weighted mean of training responses; isolated/absent -> zero); subclasses BaseBaseline (reuses B-decode) but takes per-row target symbols. (2) UntypedGraphEncoder — homogeneous GCNConv over all PPI edges collapsed to one relation, no evidence types, no condition gate; returns (h_graph,None,None) so EGIPGModel + StageALoss._graph(None) don't crash. (3) StaticTypedGraphEncoder — full TypedGraphEncoder with the condition gate pinned to 1.0 (overrides only _gate -> new_ones), §10.6 nested member #2, condition-invariant by construction. Both neural encoders honour forward(target_genes,conditions,h_do)->(h_graph,edge_gates,edge_confidences) so they train through the existing Stage-A Trainer. Tests: src/tests/test_graph_baselines.py (7) — diffusion decay from multiple sources, disconnected/absent->zero, from_hetero_graph shapes, output-schema roundtrip; untyped GCN forward shapes + no gates + condition-invariance; typed-static gates==1.0 + condition-invariant + differs from dynamic; both neural encoders emit the output schema via EGIPGModel. Adversarial workflow review (11 agents): 3 findings confirmed+fixed, correctness-critical dims clean — docs/reviews/2026-07-16-code-review-module7.md. Real-data smoke (A100, blocked-target-OOD): untyped-GNN trained+scored (systema 0.375); typed encoder OOMs 80GB on real dense subgraphs at batch 32, fits at batch 4 / expandable_segments. ./init.sh green 159 tests. Spec docs/specs/2026-07-16-module7-screening.md. · xhigh /code-review (2026-07-16): 15 findings fixed across Tiers 1-4 (9db57ae/32fb473/4e25f4b/04e6148) — registry distinct-config cap, valid summary.json, non-zero exit on all-failed wave, NETWORK-PROP SCORING PATH (score_network_propagation + run_screening extra_scorers), seed-namespaced ckpts, gpu_hours audit, MAX_COMPARATOR_FAMILIES=2, seeded_init(seed) weight-init reproducibility, one-pass val scoring, CSR train_mean, shared response_metric_suite, duplicate-name guard. ./init.sh green at 171 tests. docs/reviews/2026-07-16-code-review-module7.md. THROUGHPUT REFACTOR (2026-07-17): UntypedGraphEncoder.forward + StaticTypedGraphEncoder's inherited TypedGraphEncoder.forward are now PyG-mini-batched, and the shared neighborhood sampler got a CSR neighbour index (581 -> 26 ms/row). Untyped equivalence is pinned against its own encode_one oracle (test_untyped_batched_forward_matches_per_sample_loop); StaticTypedGraphEncoder still overrides ONLY _gate and its batched gates are all 1.0 (test_batched_static_encoder_pins_gate_to_one). Behaviour unchanged, 9.4x faster; see feat-011 evidence for the full measurement. REVIEW (2026-07-17, xhigh /code-review of ef8c1ad, 37 agents -> 35 verified -> 15 distinct defects, ALL FIXED; full record in docs/specs/2026-07-17-graph-throughput-minibatch.md). The refactor was functionally correct as shipped, and the review found what the equivalence tests could not see. (1) REAL REGRESSION: h_graph was preallocated torch.zeros(...) = float32, where the torch.stack it replaced inherited the readout's dtype -- under torch.autocast the bf16 readout was cast back up into an fp32 h_graph, so the graph pathway left the autocast region at a different dtype from every other pathway; now allocated with pooled.dtype, and the all-absent early return (no readout runs) mirrors h_do.dtype, the tensor ProgramDecoder concatenates it with. (2) TEST BLINDNESS, the important one: _grow's order-restoring torch.sort is the single line the bit-identity claim rests on and it READS redundant, but _PRIORITY_BONUS adds 1e6 to a float32 score (spacing 0.0625 at 1e6) so scores quantise into ties in bulk and the tie-break order decides which neighbours survive the cap; deleting it left all 237 tests GREEN while the sampler diverged from the oracle on 36/600 (gene,cap,hops) combos and 35 of the fixture's 60 genes -- the four hand-picked probe genes were exactly the ones that cannot fail, and the module docstring asserted the OPPOSITE of incident()'s real contract, inviting the deletion. Fixed by sweeping EVERY fixture gene (the suite now catches that mutation), correcting the docstring, and labelling both sorts load-bearing. (3) VACUOUS TEST: allclose(g, ones_like(g)) is True on a zero-length gate, so a forward returning empty gates for every sample passed green -- which would zero StageALoss._graph's sparsity+unsourced terms and empty the Module-4 rationale with H2b still reported; now pinned to the sub-graph's real edge counts. (4) CACHE STALENESS: the CSR index was cached on the graph with no invalidation, so an appended edge was silently never seen (the full scan it replaced re-read edge_index every call) -- a rewired-network or edge-ablation control would have been cut from the pre-edit topology; fixed with a (data_ptr, shape, _version) fingerprint that rebuilds on change. (5) torch.split handed out VIEWS into the batch-wide gate tensor where the per-sample loop gave independent storage -> .clone(). (6) positional indexing narrowed input from any iterable to a sequence -> list(). (7) _gather's torch.arange ignored the graph's device (latent). Hot-path waste removed (ea[eids, col] instead of materialising every column; _induce's always-true src conjunct; 4n empty tensors -> one shared blank) took sampling 26 -> 22 ms/row and the end-to-end figure from 9.4x to 10.9x: 667 -> 61 ms/row, epoch 3.94 h -> 0.36 h, GPU util median 1% -> 46% (p90 94%). Every fix is red-green verified by reverting it and watching the matching test fail. ./init.sh green at 242. Real-graph subgraph shapes re-verified UNCHANGED (min 465 / max 512 nodes, mean 30,751 PP edges). LESSON: hand-picked probe cases are a way of choosing what the test cannot see -- mutate the one line the central claim rests on, not just the ones you thought of. REVIEW FOLLOW-UP (2026-07-17): the 2 findings first waved through are now fixed too, so all 15 are resolved. (a) incident()'s duplicate-free precondition was docstring-only -> now enforced (a repeated node re-emits its whole CSR row, so its edges return once per repeat and the sub-graph silently carries duplicates the boolean scan never produced; the check is <=512 nodes against a ~160k-edge gather, i.e. free). (b) EVAL PEAK MEMORY was a real regression, not inherent as first argued: the per-row loop bounded eval peak to ONE subgraph REGARDLESS of batch size, and screening.collect_predictions defaults to batch_size=config.BATCH_SIZE=64 under no_grad while the typed encoder OOMs 80GB at batch 32 on real dense subgraphs -- and the original goal had explicitly asked for 'a working batch size / chunking path' that was never built. Both encoders now message-pass at most config.GRAPH_ENCODE_CHUNK (default 8, env- overridable) subgraphs at once and stitch the results, so a caller's batch_size sets the OPTIMISATION batch, not the memory ceiling. MEASURED at batch 64 under no_grad on the real graph: peak 12.53 GB -> 2.01 GB (6.2x) and slightly FASTER (2.9s -> 2.5s). Chunking is invisible to results: every chunk size (1/2/3/8/off) agrees with the unchunked batch edge-for-edge (test_encode_chunking_does_not_change_results) and test_chunking_bounds_message_passing_width asserts no more than `chunk` subgraphs are ever in flight; teeth verified by injecting 4 defects (never-chunk, reversed stitch, mis-assigned gates, dropped guard), each caught. HONEST LIMIT: chunking does NOT reduce TRAINING memory -- autograd retains every chunk's activations until backward, so a batch-64 training step still peaks at 55.9 GB; batch size remains the training knob and chunking bounds evaluation. Steady-state sampling unchanged at 22 ms/row (the new guard costs nothing measurable); ./init.sh green at 252.

features.name: EG-IPG Model Implementation
features.description: Implement the full EG-IPG architecture: perturbation encoder, typed condition-gated graph encoder, program decoder with residual pathway, and sparse predictive-rationale head
features.evidence: IN-PROGRESS (2026-07-15): Module 3 program decoder + full M1+M2+M3 wiring scaffolded (delivered with the feat-005 Module-3 goal). src/tcell_pipeline/programs/program_decoder.py (ProgramDecoder: graph path Linear(512,K) + expression-only path Linear(256,K), sigmoid mixture gate lambda in [0,1], softplus uncertainty sigma, gene decode delta_x = B @ delta_z^T + r with B a FROZEN registered_buffer, not a Parameter). src/tcell_pipeline/model.py (EGIPGModel wraps PerturbationEncoder + TypedGraphEncoder + ProgramDecoder; graph_encoder=None yields the expression-only nested variant with lambda pinned to 0). Real-data smoke run_module3_smoke.py PASSED end-to-end (M1->M2->M3 on 4 real perturbations: shapes ok, finite, lambda in [0,1], sigma>0). REMAINING: Module 4 sparse predictive-rationale head, training losses, and train/calibration loops. UPDATE (2026-07-16): Module 4 sparse PREDICTIVE-rationale head built (Stage B — fitted AFTER the H1 predictor freeze; a predictive rationale, NOT a causal mechanism, deletion scores are fixed-model perturbation tests). New package src/tcell_pipeline/rationale/: RationaleHead (edge importance imp = alpha_bar * sigmoid(Linear([h_u||h_v||f_e])), zero-init scorer so an untrained head ranks by the frozen condition gate = faithful by construction; top-k selection across all 4 relations; output labelled 'predictive_rationale', never 'causal'); RationaleLoss (sparsity |S| + sufficiency ||dz_S - dz_full||^2 + necessity hinge on ||dz_\S - dz_full|| + contrastive vs matched-random; differentiable to the head via soft gate weights); FaithfulnessTester (fixed-model deletion tests: sufficiency/necessity re-run the FROZEN encoder with the rationale kept/removed, plus structural_ood_audit reporting degree/components/sparsity/hop-distance before-vs-after); MatchedRandomSampler (controls matched on per-relation edge count -> size + relation composition). Module-2 enabler: TypedGraphEncoder.encode_subgraph(...) added to expose final node states + accept a per-edge gate mask; encode_one unchanged (delegates to it, 3-tuple contract preserved). config: RATIONALE_TOP_K=15, RATIONALE_TAU=0.5, LAMBDA_SPARSE=0.01/LAMBDA_SUFF=1.0/LAMBDA_NEC=1.0/LAMBDA_CONTRAST=0.5, N_MATCHED_CONTROLS=100. Verified: 9 new synthetic tests (src/tests/test_rationale.py: imp in [0,1], top-k sorted, sufficiency<matched-random, necessity>matched-random, matched-random size+relation match, structural_ood dict, loss components computable+gradients, expr-only (no graph)->empty rationale, label 'predictive_rationale' not 'causal'); ./init.sh green at 78 tests (69 prior + 9 new). Real-data smoke run_module4_smoke.py PASSED on the real PPI graph (A1BG neighbourhood: sufficiency<matched-random, necessity>matched-random, labelled predictive_rationale). Perf: this 64-core box thrashes torch's thread pool on tiny per-subgraph GNN ops (2.5s->20ms/encode), so the CPU-only Module-4 tests + smoke pin torch.set_num_threads(1). (2) CORRECTNESS — structural_ood_audit sparsity is now PP-scoped to match its degree/component/hop metrics (was counting complex_membership in numerator+denominator while the connectivity metrics are protein-protein only). (3) TEST — the tautological structural-audit assertion (after>=0, always true) now checks the deleted-fraction equals an independently-counted removed-PP fraction + component-count monotonicity under deletion. Cleanups: sufficiency/necessity take an optional precomputed dz_full (skips the mask-invariant full re-encode across matched-random controls) exposed via a public delta_z(); _select uses torch.topk over the pooled edges (was a per-edge float() device-sync + full O(E log E) sort on ~33k edges); faithfulness imports _PP_RELATIONS from rationale_head (was a 4th duplicate); _pp_edges vectorised via edge_index.t().tolist(); run_module4_smoke uses the public delta_z/sufficiency API. Kept as spec-mandated (not defects): RationaleHead's edge_attrs param + subgraph_edges output (both in the Module 4 spec signature). ./init.sh green at 79 tests (+1 determinism regression over the 78 above); Module 4 real-data smoke re-run PASSED. UPDATE (2026-07-16, Module 5 — Loss + Training; walkthrough §8): the four model modules are now TRAINABLE. New package src/tcell_pipeline/training/: losses.py (StageALoss trains M1+M2+M3 = Huber response program+gene [delta=1] + focal-BCE DEHead up/down [Linear(256,2G), gamma=2, labels from |zscore|>=1.645 as the adj_p<0.1 proxy the dataset contract carries] + donor-invariance f_shared=Linear(K,K) + edge-gate L_graph [sparsity sum|alpha_bar| + unsourced sum((1-conf)*alpha^2), conf defaults 0]; StageBCalibrationLoss = Gaussian NLL, a LOSS MODULE ONLY per §8.1 [fitted after H1 freeze, no loop]; DEHead); dataset.py (PerturbationDataset: split-aware via blocked_target_ood.csv, __getitem__ -> (batch_dict, target, condition, delta_z_true, delta_x_true, row_index); features q_pre-only [q_post fence held downstream]; delta_z_true = program_response A for train rows else z@B projection out-of-fold; delta_x_true = zscore.npz row; + collate); trainer.py (Trainer: AdamW lr=1e-3/wd=1e-5 over model AND loss params, frozen basis B [persistent=False buffer] neither optimised nor checkpointed, grad-clip 1.0, patience-10 early stop, atomic best+last checkpoints to data/checkpoints/, per-epoch loss components to data/logs/); run_train.py (Stage A orchestrator on real marts, CLI --lr/--epochs/--batch-size/--seed/--n-max/--expr-only, pins set_num_threads(1)). RationaleLoss (Module 4) NOT reimplemented. config: LR/WEIGHT_DECAY/MAX_EPOCHS/EARLY_STOP_PATIENCE/BATCH_SIZE/GRAD_CLIP/HUBER_DELTA/FOCAL_GAMMA/LAMBDA_DE/LAMBDA_INV/LAMBDA_GRAPH/LAMBDA_GENE/DE_CALL_ZSCORE/CHECKPOINTS_ROOT/LOGS_ROOT. Verified: 8 new synthetic tests (src/tests/test_training.py: Stage A component shapes+gradient flow to h_do/DE head/f_shared, graph-gate penalty [confidence lowers unsourced term], Stage B Gaussian NLL+grad, DE probs in [0,1], learnable lambda mixture, dataset keys+q_post fence+program_response-vs-projection dz source, 2-epoch checkpointed run, param-update); ./init.sh green at 87 tests (79 prior + 8 new), zero warnings. Real-data Stage A smoke PASSED both ways: expr-only (256 examples x3 epochs, best_val 3.48) and full-graph M1->M2->M3 (n_max 4 x1 epoch, L_graph active on real edge_gates) train + back-prop + write atomic checkpoints. Design+as-built: docs/specs/2026-07-16-module5-training.md. POST-REVIEW (2026-07-16, adversarial workflow review of the Module 5 diff — 3 dimensions [loss-math/data-leakage/training-loop] -> per-finding verify; loss-math clean, 1 refuted [false trainer device-mismatch: moot CPU-only + encoders self-place], 1 CONFIRMED and documented not silently patched): L_invariance is INERT on the real marts — Module 0 averages donor PCs to condition-level means (control_profiles), so there are no per-donor rows and the spec's donor-pair objective is vacuously satisfied (no donor variation to be invariant to). The (target,condition) key is the CORRECT donor-invariance grouping; the sole artefact is an upstream id_mapping paralog collision (GPR89A/GPR89B -> 'GPHRA', 6/33983 rows, negligible at lambda_inv) which is a feat-002 concern, not a loss defect. Resolution: machinery kept (spec-required, forward-compatible, unit-tested on synthetic donor groups) with a ponytail: ceiling marker; activates the moment a per-donor axis is reintroduced upstream. UPDATE (2026-07-16, INTELLIGENT donor-invariance fix — supersedes the 'documented-inert' resolution above): the individual donors were NOT gone — they survive in control_donor_profiles.parquet (4 real donors CE0006864/CE0008162/CE0008678/CE0010866 x 3 conditions; the training mart's donor_pc is exactly their per-condition mean, verified). L_invariance was reformulated from the inert (target,condition) row-grouping to real DONOR RESAMPLING: the Trainer re-runs the encoder under DONOR_INVARIANCE_SAMPLES distinct real donor PC vectors per row (with the encoder in eval so DropEdge doesn't contaminate the donor signal; gradients still flow) and StageALoss penalises the VARIANCE of f_shared(delta_z) across those donors (delta_z = shared + donor-nuisance, spec §8.2 item 4). This is a real, dense donor-generalisation signal on every example, and it OPTIMISES: on a real 128-example expr-only run the term falls 1.30 -> 0.25 in one epoch. New: dataset.load_donor_pool + sample_donor_variants; Trainer donor_invariance/donor_samples + _donor_variants; config DONOR_INVARIANCE=True, DONOR_INVARIANCE_SAMPLES=2; run_train --no-donor-invariance opt-out. ponytail: donor resampling ~triples the CPU-bound graph Stage-A cost (each variant re-runs the donor-independent message passing) -> cache node states + re-run only readout+decoder to remove it. Verified: test_training.py grew to 11 tests (+3: invariance-zero-without-variants, donor pool+resampler distinct-donors, real donor-invariance signal non-zero+trains f_shared on / clean 0 off); ./init.sh green at 90 tests (79 prior + 11), zero warnings; expr-only real run (128x2, invariance 1.30->0.25) + full-graph real runs (donor-invariance active, and --no-donor-invariance for the L_graph check) all train + back-prop + checkpoint. The paralogue HGNC collision noted above (GPR89A/GPR89B->GPHRA) is an upstream feat-002 id_mapping item, now independent of L_invariance. POST-REVIEW ROUND 2 (2026-07-16, xhigh /code-review of 6dcf196..HEAD -- 6 finder angles + per-(file,line) verify -> 15 verified defects; confirmed correctness ones fixed): (1) CRITICAL -- the donor-resampling L_invariance was DEGENERATE: penalising Var(f_shared(delta_z)) with a free f_shared=Linear(K,K) is globally minimised at W=0 (AdamW weight_decay + the variance objective both drive W->0), so the term decayed back to inert without pressuring the encoder -- the same inertness in a new guise. FIXED: dropped f_shared, penalise Var(delta_z) DIRECTLY (no collapsible projection; forces the encoder to emit donor-invariant delta_z). Re-verified on real data: the term now optimises VIA THE ENCODER, 2.15 -> 0.19 over 3 epochs. (2) the stochastic donor term leaked into VALIDATION -> val_total non-deterministic -> early-stopping/best-checkpoint partly RNG. FIXED: donor variants computed in TRAIN ONLY; val invariance == 0, val metric deterministic. (3) donor invariance silently no-op'd when control_donor_profiles.parquet absent (not in run_train's required gate) while the log printed True. FIXED: profile parquet added to required when donor_invariance is on (fail-fast) + honest on/off log + warning. (4) L_graph summed over batch x edges while other terms are means -> batch-size/density-dependent weight. FIXED: mean-reduced over the batch. (5) Trainer.__init__ torch.manual_seed(seed) global reseed side effect. FIXED: dedicated torch.Generators (donor resample + a seeded DataLoader shuffle), no global-RNG mutation. (6) empty training split -> opaque RandomSampler/KeyError crash. FIXED: clear ValueError on 0-example train set. (7) DEHead sized from global config.H_DO_DIM not the wrapped decoder. FIXED: h_do_dim = model.decoder.h_do_dim. Plus cheap guards (de_obs<->pc row-count check) + DRY (DONOR_COLS reused from encoders/batch). Verified: test_training.py 11 -> 13 (+graph batch-normalization, +empty-split guard; donor-invariance test now asserts train non-zero / val 0 / off 0 + no f_shared attr); ./init.sh green at 92 tests (79 prior + 13), zero warnings; expr-only real run (256x3) invariance 2.15->0.19 with val invariance 0. FLAGGED not silently changed: (a) delta_z_true is the sparse-PCA score A for train rows but the z@B projection out of fold -- a feat-005 modeling decision (persist the fitted sparse-coder for consistent val targets, or use z@B everywhere); (b) edge_confidences never wired into L_graph so its unsourced term stays a plain L2 (thread per-edge source confidence from the graph output to make it source-aware); (c) getattr(train_ds,'donor_pool',{}) silently disables donor invariance under a Subset wrapper. Refuted: a claimed trainer device mismatch (encoders self-place their sub-outputs). ROUND 3 (2026-07-16, implemented the 3 items round 2 flagged): (a) delta_z_true MISMATCH FIXED -- z@B for EVERY row (one consistent fold-local target); the sparse-PCA A / program_response is no longer a training-target dependency (removed from PerturbationDataset + run_train's required gate), so the val loss now measures the trained objective. (b) edge_confidences WIRED -- the per-edge source confidence (edge-feature score column, clipped [0,1]) is threaded TypedGraphEncoder._edges_with_gates -> encode_subgraph (edge_confidences key) -> forward (3-tuple h_graph,edge_gates,edge_confidences) -> EGIPGModel.forward out['edge_confidences'] -> Trainer -> StageALoss._graph, so the unsourced-reliance term down-weights well-sourced edges instead of treating all edges as unsourced (real data: L_graph 21148 -> 17977 once confidences applied; verified aligned per-edge to gates, all in [0,1]). New encoder callers updated (model.forward, run_module2_smoke, test_graph forward unpacking). (c) Subset silent-disable FIXED -- Trainer._resolve_donor_pool walks wrapper .dataset chains so a wrapped train set doesn't silently drop donor invariance. Verified: full-graph Stage A back-props through the confidence-weighted L_graph (exit 0); test_graph asserts edge_confidences aligned+clipped; dataset dz test asserts z@B for both train and val; ./init.sh green at 92 tests, zero warnings. REMAINING for feat-008 done: the Stage-B calibration + rationale FIT loops (both loss modules exist, no fit loop), the near-null-signal freeze gate, and feat-007 (graph baselines) still not-started. CORRECTION (2026-07-17): the sentence above -- "feat-007 (graph baselines) is still not-started" -- was true when written (2026-07-15) and is now STALE: feat-007 was completed 2026-07-16 (status 'done'; src/tcell_pipeline/baselines/graph_baselines.py, three PPI-graph references). features.name: Evaluation Metrics
features.description: Implement MAE, RMSE, Pearson/Spearman correlation, Systema perturbation-specific metrics, centroid accuracy, top-k recall, and signed-DE F1 with two independent implementations that agree on a fixture
features.evidence: DONE (2026-07-16, Module 6 — Evaluation Metrics; walkthrough §10, report §Evaluation Metrics). New package src/tcell_pipeline/evaluation/: metrics.py — 10 fns across the 8 metric groups, each computed PER ROW then MACRO-averaged (a row = one perturbation-target x condition response, so per-row IS per-perturbation; deliberately not micro/pooled): mae, rmse, pearson_corr, spearman_corr, systema_pert_specific_delta (primary H1 endpoint §10.2: corr(pred-train_mean, true-train_mean) — a treatment-mean-only predictor scores ~0), centroid_accuracy (prediction cosine-closest to its OWN true centroid vs others), topk_recall (strongest-|mag| up/down genes), sign_accuracy (sign of strongest effects), program_cosine, signed_de_metrics (macro-F1 + per-class precision/recall + AUPRC for up/down; AUROC omitted per report). Zero/constant/non-finite convention: a row with no signal contributes 0.0 (a zero predictor scores worst — the G2-MQ requirement). metrics_ref.py — a SECOND, independent implementation (per-row loops + scipy/sklearn) of mae/rmse/pearson/spearman/systema/centroid/program_cosine that AGREES with metrics.py on a fixed fixture AND on zero/constant/non-finite rows (report: 'two independent implementations on a fixed fixture'). metric_qualification.py — the G2-MQ model-blind gate (§10.1): qualify_metric(fn, neg_controls, pos_refs) passes IFF max(negatives) < min(positives); control constructors zero / perturbed-mean / label-permutation-N1 (a DERANGEMENT) / row-shuffle-N2 + oracle / guide-split-half positives, values accepted as scalars, (pred,true) tuples, or callables. control_reference.py (§10.5 safeguards): independent_control_metric (ctrl_a off prediction, ctrl_b off truth) vs shared_control_diagnostic (bias diagnostic only) + null_control_predictor (~0 under the corrected estimator). config: METRICS_TOP_K=20, METRICS_SIGN_TOP_N=50. Metrics accept numpy or torch (CPU/CUDA via .detach().cpu().numpy()). Verified: src/tests/test_metrics.py (23 tests: each metric known-answer, two-impl agreement on a non-degenerate fixture + on zero/constant rows + on non-finite rows, G2-MQ ordering pass & fail, label-perm derangement floor, §10.5 null-control neutrality under independent control + shared-control inflation, signed-DE perfect + imperfect-probs discrimination, magnitude-selection for topk/sign, output-schema roundtrip); ./init.sh green at 131 tests, zero warnings. Design+as-built: docs/specs/2026-07-16-module6-evaluation.md. POST-REVIEW (2026-07-16, adversarial workflow review of the Module 6 diff — 6 dimensions x per-finding adversarial verify, 8/8 findings CONFIRMED then fixed; docs/reviews/2026-07-16-code-review-module6.md): (1) centroid_accuracy credited a degenerate zero predictor 1.0 (tied at cosine 0 with every centroid counted as a hit) -> guard added: a zero-norm/non-finite prediction row is a MISS. (2) metrics.py silently returned 0.0 on non-finite rows while metrics_ref returned NaN (and crashed sklearn's finite-check in centroid) -> both now contribute 0.0 BY CONSTRUCTION (finite-guarded row reducers; spearman masks non-finite pre-rank; centroid sanitises the sklearn input) — a new non-finite agreement test locks it. (3) label_permutation is now a derangement so N1 reliably hits the null floor. (6/7/8) weak tests upgraded: magnitude-selection cases for topk/sign, agreement extended to degenerate+non-finite rows, imperfect-probs signed-DE discrimination. POST-REVIEW ROUND 2 (2026-07-16, xhigh workflow-backed /code-review of commit 9f4f9d6 — 12 verified findings [7 CONFIRMED correctness + 2 PLAUSIBLE + 3 cleanup], all fixed; docs/reviews round-2 section): the two 'must-agree' implementations diverged on cases round 1 never exercised (round 1 only corrupted pred, never true): (1) centroid_accuracy collapsed the whole fold to 0.0 when TRUE/bank held a non-finite value -> _cosine_matrix now sanitises non-finite bank entries to 0 (matching metrics_ref). (2) centroid_accuracy's own-vs-bank normalisation was inconsistent -- the bank floored norms at 1e-12 so a tiny-norm prediction pointing at the WRONG centroid scored a spurious 1.0 -> proper zero-norm masking, no floor, so own and bank share one normalisation. (3) metrics_ref's constant guard std==0 is FP-fragile (a genuinely-constant row at ~2000 genes has std~1e-16, slipping the guard -> scipy NaN poisons the macro-average) -> both impls now gate degeneracy on max==min (exact, representation-independent). (4) product-form sqrt(sum(p^2)*sum(t^2)) underflowed for tiny-magnitude rows where per-vector norms don't -> separate roots sqrt(sum(p^2))*sqrt(sum(t^2)). (5) topk_recall/sign_accuracy lacked the higher-is-better degenerate/non-finite->0.0 convention -> a diverged/zero predictor no longer earns chance recall. (8) independent_control_metric/shared_control_diagnostic now forward **metric_kwargs so the 3-arg primary endpoint composes (metric=systema_pert_specific_delta, train_mean=...). (9) mae/rmse docstring overclaim fixed: the 0.0 convention is scoped to higher-is-better metrics; the error metrics propagate non-finite (a corrupted prediction must not be rewarded with zero error). Cleanups: (10) topk_recall/sign_accuracy vectorised (argpartition axis=1); (11) row_shuffle -> rng.permuted(axis=1); (12) the copy-pasted _np helper consolidated into evaluation/_arrays.py:to_numpy (metrics_ref keeps its OWN converter -- it is the independent second implementation). +14 regression tests across metrics+baselines (test_metrics.py now 30 tests). ./init.sh green at 145 tests, zero warnings; both impls verified to agree on non-finite true, high-dimensional constant rows, tiny-norm wrong-direction predictions, and extreme-scale (no underflow collapse) under -W error.

features.name: External Comparators
features.description: Adapt Stable-Shift and TxPert-public to the same data format, splits, and output schema; document compatibility and license status
features.evidence: IN-PROGRESS (2026-07-17, Module 8 — External Comparators; report §Comparators/§protocol). Adapter framework + compatibility reports + tests DONE; the 16-trial comparator campaign on real data is the remaining compute (depends on the frozen fold + a converged model, as with feat-011). src/tcell_pipeline/comparators/: (1) StableShiftAdapter — REIMPLEMENTED (published code unconfirmed): fold-local low-rank truncated-SVD subspace fit on TRAIN responses only, then predict a held-out target's shift by one STRING graph-conv (self-loop + presence-weighted neighbour mean of the reduced coords) decoded through the low-rank basis; BaseBaseline subclass, from_hetero_graph(string_only=True), (delta_z, delta_x=dz@B.T) schema. (2) TxPertPublicAdapter — PUBLIC-ONLY (never the proprietary TxPert graph/checkpoints): a sparse graph transformer reduced to a single STRING score-attention head (softmax over edge confidence over a query's covered neighbours' training responses); records whether valence-labs/TxPert is importable but predict stays the public reimpl. source_adjacency(graph, sources=('string',)) is the shared STRING-channel adjacency builder (filters PP relations by source one-hot, weights by score, symmetrises). compatibility_report.py writes <COMPARATORS_ROOT>/<family>/compatibility_report.yaml (license, exposure class, checkpoint, explicit PUBLIC_ONLY flag, wrapped-upstream). Both register as distinct comparator families within MAX_COMPARATOR_FAMILIES=2 / MAX_COMPARATOR_TRIALS=16. config: COMPARATORS_ROOT. Tests: src/tests/test_comparators.py (6) — STRING-only source filtering excludes non-STRING edges; Stable-Shift recovers a single covered neighbour (full-rank low-rank reconstruct) + absent->zero + gene decode; TxPert single-neighbour attention == that neighbour + convex-combination bound + absent->zero; compatibility report public-only; two distinct families register; explicit PUBLIC_ONLY (not a 'public' substring of 'non-public'). Adversarial review (2026-07-17, docs/reviews/2026-07-17-code-review-module8.md): comparators finder CONFIRMED no train/challenge leakage (fit uses train responses only); 1 low finding fixed (public_only substring -> explicit flag). ./init.sh green at 200 tests. Spec docs/specs/2026-07-17-module8-comparators-audit-sealed-repro.md. ponytail: single graph-conv hop / fixed SVD rank / single attention head / STRING-only — documented upgrades: more hops, learned propagation, multi-head STRING+GO transformer, or wrapping the upstream public checkpoint. REMAINING for done: the real-data comparator run (fit on real STRING, score the real challenge fold) + registered 16-trial budget. · xhigh /code-review pass 2 (2026-07-17, 63 agents, docs/reviews/2026-07-17-code-review-module8.md): 1 confirmed finding on comparators FIXED — TxPertPublicAdapter.wrapped was bound from mere importability of any module named 'txpert' (real package, name-squat, or a stray txpert.py on sys.path), so the compatibility_report.yaml asserted wrapped_upstream=true in the one artifact whose purpose is provenance audit, while predict() is the public reimplementation on EVERY path and never calls upstream. FIXED: wrapped=False always; importability recorded separately as upstream_importable. ./init.sh green at 215 tests. THROUGHPUT REFACTOR (2026-07-17, graph mini-batch goal): the compute ceiling that blocked this feature's real-data campaign is LIFTED. Profiling the real 25,440-node graph on an A100 showed the deferred task's premise was wrong: message passing was only 5% of wall-clock (34 ms/row) while sample_subgraph was 95% (581 ms/row), because _grow/_induce scanned the whole ~8M-edge table PER ROW (torch.isin alone = 59% of sampler time, _induce's remap gather 28%, argsort 0.03%). Fixed at root cause in order: (1) neighborhood_sampler._NeighborIndex -- a per-relation CSR built ONCE per graph (0.85 s, ~130 MB), so incident-edge lookup is O(sum degree) not O(|E|): 581 -> 26 ms/row; (2) then PyG Batch.from_data_list mini-batching of the now-dominant message passing in TypedGraphEncoder.forward + UntypedGraphEncoder.forward (condition gate scattered per edge via the batch vector so each edge is gated by ITS OWN sample's condition; readout uses to_dense_batch + key_padding_mask so attention cannot leak across targets and still sums to 1 per sample; gates/confidences split back to the per-sample list contract Module 4 and StageALoss._graph consume). MEASURED on an A100, real graph, forward+backward, bs=8: 667 -> 71 ms/row (9.4x); 21,262-row epoch 3.94 h -> 0.42 h; GPU utilisation median 1% -> 43% (p90 86%). bs=8 is the sweet spot (bs=32 = 15.2 vs 14.0 rows/s for 31.5 vs 10.0 GB). CORRECTNESS: exact-equivalence tests pin both halves -- the sampler is bit-identical to the frozen full-scan oracle (real-graph subgraphs unchanged: min 465 / max 512 nodes, mean 30,751 PP edges) and batched forward matches the per-sample loop edge-for-edge on CPU AND CUDA; oracles are self-contained (importing the sampler's policy constants would move both sides and silently void the test -- caught and fixed during this work); teeth verified by injecting 6 defects, each caught. DropEdge is train-only/random so equivalence is asserted in eval(). NOT saturated and the reason is known: the batch is still sampled row-by-row on CPU (~26 ms/row, ~37% of a step) -- next ceiling is batch-aware sampling / per-target subgraph cache / DataLoader workers (deliberately not done, YAGNI). ./init.sh green at 236 (224 + 12). Real-data smoke, 600 rows / 1 epoch, machinery only: untyped_gnn systema=0.0534, typed_static systema=0.0531, condition_gated systema=0.0432. The campaign itself remains UNRUN, so this feature stays in-progress. REVIEW (2026-07-17, xhigh /code-review of ef8c1ad, 37 agents -> 35 verified -> 15 distinct defects, ALL FIXED; full record in docs/specs/2026-07-17-graph-throughput-minibatch.md). The refactor was functionally correct as shipped, and the review found what the equivalence tests could not see. (1) REAL REGRESSION: h_graph was preallocated torch.zeros(...) = float32, where the torch.stack it replaced inherited the readout's dtype -- under torch.autocast the bf16 readout was cast back up into an fp32 h_graph, so the graph pathway left the autocast region at a different dtype from every other pathway; now allocated with pooled.dtype, and the all-absent early return (no readout runs) mirrors h_do.dtype, the tensor ProgramDecoder concatenates it with. (2) TEST BLINDNESS, the important one: _grow's order-restoring torch.sort is the single line the bit-identity claim rests on and it READS redundant, but _PRIORITY_BONUS adds 1e6 to a float32 score (spacing 0.0625 at 1e6) so scores quantise into ties in bulk and the tie-break order decides which neighbours survive the cap; deleting it left all 237 tests GREEN while the sampler diverged from the oracle on 36/600 (gene,cap,hops) combos and 35 of the fixture's 60 genes -- the four hand-picked probe genes were exactly the ones that cannot fail, and the module docstring asserted the OPPOSITE of incident()'s real contract, inviting the deletion. Fixed by sweeping EVERY fixture gene (the suite now catches that mutation), correcting the docstring, and labelling both sorts load-bearing. (3) VACUOUS TEST: allclose(g, ones_like(g)) is True on a zero-length gate, so a forward returning empty gates for every sample passed green -- which would zero StageALoss._graph's sparsity+unsourced terms and empty the Module-4 rationale with H2b still reported; now pinned to the sub-graph's real edge counts. (4) CACHE STALENESS: the CSR index was cached on the graph with no invalidation, so an appended edge was silently never seen (the full scan it replaced re-read edge_index every call) -- a rewired-network or edge-ablation control would have been cut from the pre-edit topology; fixed with a (data_ptr, shape, _version) fingerprint that rebuilds on change. (5) torch.split handed out VIEWS into the batch-wide gate tensor where the per-sample loop gave independent storage -> .clone(). (6) positional indexing narrowed input from any iterable to a sequence -> list(). (7) _gather's torch.arange ignored the graph's device (latent). Hot-path waste removed (ea[eids, col] instead of materialising every column; _induce's always-true src conjunct; 4n empty tensors -> one shared blank) took sampling 26 -> 22 ms/row and the end-to-end figure from 9.4x to 10.9x: 667 -> 61 ms/row, epoch 3.94 h -> 0.36 h, GPU util median 1% -> 46% (p90 94%). Every fix is red-green verified by reverting it and watching the matching test fail. ./init.sh green at 242. Real-graph subgraph shapes re-verified UNCHANGED (min 465 / max 512 nodes, mean 30,751 PP edges). LESSON: hand-picked probe cases are a way of choosing what the test cannot see -- mutate the one line the central claim rests on, not just the ones you thought of. REVIEW FOLLOW-UP (2026-07-17): the 2 findings first waved through are now fixed too, so all 15 are resolved. (a) incident()'s duplicate-free precondition was docstring-only -> now enforced (a repeated node re-emits its whole CSR row, so its edges return once per repeat and the sub-graph silently carries duplicates the boolean scan never produced; the check is <=512 nodes against a ~160k-edge gather, i.e. free). (b) EVAL PEAK MEMORY was a real regression, not inherent as first argued: the per-row loop bounded eval peak to ONE subgraph REGARDLESS of batch size, and screening.collect_predictions defaults to batch_size=config.BATCH_SIZE=64 under no_grad while the typed encoder OOMs 80GB at batch 32 on real dense subgraphs -- and the original goal had explicitly asked for 'a working batch size / chunking path' that was never built. Both encoders now message-pass at most config.GRAPH_ENCODE_CHUNK (default 8, env- overridable) subgraphs at once and stitch the results, so a caller's batch_size sets the OPTIMISATION batch, not the memory ceiling. MEASURED at batch 64 under no_grad on the real graph: peak 12.53 GB -> 2.01 GB (6.2x) and slightly FASTER (2.9s -> 2.5s). features.evidence: IN-PROGRESS (2026-07-16, Module 7 — Screening Harness; walkthrough §10.6-10.7, report §screening lines 1109/1187/1274-1291). Harness CODE complete + tested; the full 32-trial campaign + 5-seed promotion is the remaining compute work. src/tcell_pipeline/screening/: screening.py — screen_config (train via existing Trainer -> reload best ckpt -> score val in dataset order -> write predictions [common output schema] + a 1-row metrics table -> return the metric suite; metric spaces mirror run_module6_smoke._score, primary = systema_pert_specific_delta), run_screening (screen each config on the shared split, report H2a [typed-static>expr-only] + H2b [condition-gated>typed-static] on systema, write summary.json; FAILURE-ISOLATING — an OOM/crash is logged + skipped, the rest of the wave still runs), nested_family_factories/configs (fresh model per call — fresh graph AND perturbation encoder; the review-caught weight-sharing fix). experiment_registry.py — register_run (immutable run-NNNN id; enforces the 32 EG-IPG / 16-per-comparator trial caps -> ValueError), log_run (records outcome incl FAILED — complete audit trail), YAML manifest, load_registry degrades missing/empty/null-runs to []. run_screening.py — real-data driver (nested family + untyped diagnostic wave). config: SCREENING_ROOT, REGISTRY_PATH, MAX_EGIPG_TRIALS=32, MAX_COMPARATOR_TRIALS=16, N_SCREENING_SEEDS=1, N_FINAL_SEEDS=5. Adversarial review 3 findings fixed (docs/reviews/2026-07-16-code-review-module7.md). Real-data smoke (A100, blocked-target-OOD, 40-row/1-epoch/batch-4): all 4 wave members trained+scored+registered [completed]; HONEST NEGATIVE — graph variants do NOT beat expression-only (systema 0.377 expr-only / 0.362 typed-static / 0.348 condition-gated; H2a delta=-0.015, H2b delta=-0.015, neither supported) — the near-null-signal regime, on untrained-to-convergence models. ./init.sh green 159 tests. Spec docs/specs/2026-07-16-module7-screening.md. REMAINING: the 32-trial screening campaign + neighborhood/graph-source waves + 5-seed promotion on the full split with convergent training. · xhigh /code-review (2026-07-16): 15 findings fixed across Tiers 1-4 (9db57ae/32fb473/4e25f4b/04e6148) — registry distinct-config cap, valid summary.json, non-zero exit on all-failed wave, NETWORK-PROP SCORING PATH (score_network_propagation + run_screening extra_scorers), seed-namespaced ckpts, gpu_hours audit, MAX_COMPARATOR_FAMILIES=2, seeded_init(seed) weight-init reproducibility, one-pass val scoring, CSR train_mean, shared response_metric_suite, duplicate-name guard. ./init.sh green at 171 tests. docs/reviews/2026-07-16-code-review-module7.md. THROUGHPUT REFACTOR (2026-07-17, graph mini-batch goal): the compute ceiling that blocked this feature's real-data campaign is LIFTED. Profiling the real 25,440-node graph on an A100 showed the deferred task's premise was wrong: message passing was only 5% of wall-clock (34 ms/row) while sample_subgraph was 95% (581 ms/row), because _grow/_induce scanned the whole ~8M-edge table PER ROW (torch.isin alone = 59% of sampler time, _induce's remap gather 28%, argsort 0.03%). Fixed at root cause in order: (1) neighborhood_sampler._NeighborIndex -- a per-relation CSR built ONCE per graph (0.85 s, ~130 MB), so incident-edge lookup is O(sum degree) not O(|E|): 581 -> 26 ms/row; (2) then PyG Batch.from_data_list mini-batching of the now-dominant message passing in TypedGraphEncoder.forward + UntypedGraphEncoder.forward (condition gate scattered per edge via the batch vector so each edge is gated by ITS OWN sample's condition; readout uses to_dense_batch + key_padding_mask so attention cannot leak across targets and still sums to 1 per sample; gates/confidences split back to the per-sample list contract Module 4 and StageALoss._graph consume). MEASURED on an A100, real graph, forward+backward, bs=8: 667 -> 71 ms/row (9.4x); 21,262-row epoch 3.94 h -> 0.42 h; GPU utilisation median 1% -> 43% (p90 86%). bs=8 is the sweet spot (bs=32 = 15.2 vs 14.0 rows/s for 31.5 vs 10.0 GB). CORRECTNESS: exact-equivalence tests pin both halves -- the sampler is bit-identical to the frozen full-scan oracle (real-graph subgraphs unchanged: min 465 / max 512 nodes, mean 30,751 PP edges) and batched forward matches the per-sample loop edge-for-edge on CPU AND CUDA; oracles are self-contained (importing the sampler's policy constants would move both sides and silently void the test -- caught and fixed during this work); teeth verified by injecting 6 defects, each caught. DropEdge is train-only/random so equivalence is asserted in eval(). NOT saturated and the reason is known: the batch is still sampled row-by-row on CPU (~26 ms/row, ~37% of a step) -- next ceiling is batch-aware sampling / per-target subgraph cache / DataLoader workers (deliberately not done, YAGNI). ./init.sh green at 236 (224 + 12). Real-data smoke, 600 rows / 1 epoch, machinery only: untyped_gnn systema=0.0534, typed_static systema=0.0531, condition_gated systema=0.0432. The campaign itself remains UNRUN, so this feature stays in-progress. REVIEW (2026-07-17, xhigh /code-review of ef8c1ad, 37 agents -> 35 verified -> 15 distinct defects, ALL FIXED; full record in docs/specs/2026-07-17-graph-throughput-minibatch.md). The refactor was functionally correct as shipped, and the review found what the equivalence tests could not see. (1) REAL REGRESSION: h_graph was preallocated torch.zeros(...) = float32, where the torch.stack it replaced inherited the readout's dtype -- under torch.autocast the bf16 readout was cast back up into an fp32 h_graph, so the graph pathway left the autocast region at a different dtype from every other pathway; now allocated with pooled.dtype, and the all-absent early return (no readout runs) mirrors h_do.dtype, the tensor ProgramDecoder concatenates it with. (2) TEST BLINDNESS, the important one: _grow's order-restoring torch.sort is the single line the bit-identity claim rests on and it READS redundant, but _PRIORITY_BONUS adds 1e6 to a float32 score (spacing 0.0625 at 1e6) so scores quantise into ties in bulk and the tie-break order decides which neighbours survive the cap; deleting it left all 237 tests GREEN while the sampler diverged from the oracle on 36/600 (gene,cap,hops) combos and 35 of the fixture's 60 genes -- the four hand-picked probe genes were exactly the ones that cannot fail, and the module docstring asserted the OPPOSITE of incident()'s real contract, inviting the deletion. Fixed by sweeping EVERY fixture gene (the suite now catches that mutation), correcting the docstring, and labelling both sorts load-bearing. (3) VACUOUS TEST: allclose(g, ones_like(g)) is True on a zero-length gate, so a forward returning empty gates for every sample passed green -- which would zero StageALoss._graph's sparsity+unsourced terms and empty the Module-4 rationale with H2b still reported; now pinned to the sub-graph's real edge counts. (4) CACHE STALENESS: the CSR index was cached on the graph with no invalidation, so an appended edge was silently never seen (the full scan it replaced re-read edge_index every call) -- a rewired-network or edge-ablation control would have been cut from the pre-edit topology; fixed with a (data_ptr, shape, _version) fingerprint that rebuilds on change. (5) torch.split handed out VIEWS into the batch-wide gate tensor where the per-sample loop gave independent storage -> .clone(). (6) positional indexing narrowed input from any iterable to a sequence -> list(). (7) _gather's torch.arange ignored the graph's device (latent). Hot-path waste removed (ea[eids, col] instead of materialising every column; _induce's always-true src conjunct; 4n empty tensors -> one shared blank) took sampling 26 -> 22 ms/row and the end-to-end figure from 9.4x to 10.9x: 667 -> 61 ms/row, epoch 3.94 h -> 0.36 h, GPU util median 1% -> 46% (p90 94%). Every fix is red-green verified by reverting it and watching the matching test fail. ./init.sh green at 242. Real-graph subgraph shapes re-verified UNCHANGED (min 465 / max 512 nodes, mean 30,751 PP edges). LESSON: hand-picked probe cases are a way of choosing what the test cannot see -- mutate the one line the central claim rests on, not just the ones you thought of. REVIEW FOLLOW-UP (2026-07-17): the 2 findings first waved through are now fixed too, so all 15 are resolved. (a) incident()'s duplicate-free precondition was docstring-only -> now enforced (a repeated node re-emits its whole CSR row, so its edges return once per repeat and the sub-graph silently carries duplicates the boolean scan never produced; the check is <=512 nodes against a ~160k-edge gather, i.e. free). (b) EVAL PEAK MEMORY was a real regression, not inherent as first argued: the per-row loop bounded eval peak to ONE subgraph REGARDLESS of batch size, and screening.collect_predictions defaults to batch_size=config.BATCH_SIZE=64 under no_grad while the typed encoder OOMs 80GB at batch 32 on real dense subgraphs -- and the original goal had explicitly asked for 'a working batch size / chunking path' that was never built. Both encoders now message-pass at most config.GRAPH_ENCODE_CHUNK (default 8, env- overridable) subgraphs at once and stitch the results, so a caller's batch_size sets the OPTIMISATION batch, not the memory ceiling. MEASURED at batch 64 under no_grad on the real graph: peak 12.53 GB -> 2.01 GB (6.2x) and slightly FASTER (2.9s -> 2.5s). Chunking is invisible to results: every chunk size (1/2/3/8/off) agrees with the unchunked batch edge-for-edge (test_encode_chunking_does_not_change_results) and test_chunking_bounds_message_passing_width asserts no more than `chunk` subgraphs are ever in flight; teeth verified by injecting 4 defects (never-chunk, reversed stitch, mis-assigned gates, dropped guard), each caught. HONEST LIMIT: chunking does NOT reduce TRAINING memory -- autograd retains every chunk's activations until backward, so a batch-64 training step still peaks at 55.9 GB; batch size remains the training knob and chunking bounds evaluation. Steady-state sampling unchanged at 22 ms/row (the new guard costs nothing measurable); ./init.sh green at 252. CAMPAIGN INFRASTRUCTURE + LAUNCH (2026-07-17): the feat-011 full-fold campaign is RUNNING (launched 19:20 IST, ~12.5 h ETA, run_screening_campaign.sh under nohup) -- the nested family on ONE shared FULL fold (21,262 train / 4,400 val), 20-epoch budget, bs=8, one lane per A100 at PCI 0/1/4 (GPU 2 held by another tenant, index 3 is a 4GB T400). RESULTS NOT IN YET; H2a/H2b + promotion land in docs/specs/2026-07-17-feat011-full-fold-campaign.md. CORRECTION (2026-07-17): the '0.36 h/epoch' recorded above for the graph throughput refactor is an ENCODER-ONLY bench (bench.py) and does NOT size a real training run -- Trainer._donor_variants re-forwards the whole model once per donor variant, so a real step is 1+DONOR_INVARIANCE_SAMPLES=3 forwards. Measured on the real fold: condition_gated 183.4 ms/row = 3.0x the 61 ms/row bench, i.e. a 20-epoch campaign was 22.7 h, not the ~7 h that number implies. FIX: per-target subgraph cache (_SubgraphCache, config.SUBGRAPH_CACHE_SIZE, bounded LRU on the graph like the CSR index). sample_subgraph is a pure function of (graph, gene, hops, cap) -- independent of donor, weights, and train/eval mode -- so it is memoisable, whereas NODE STATES are not (the main forward runs DropEdge-on and the variants DropEdge-off, and states are a function of weights that change every step). Measured steady state, real fold, bs=8: condition_gated train 183.4 -> 102.8 ms/row, val 42.2 -> 13.0; typed_static 158.3 -> 80.3; untyped_gnn 95.7 -> 18.1 (5.3x -- it was almost pure sampling); expression_only unchanged. 1.8x end-to-end: 22.7 h -> 12.5 h fanned over 3 A100s (~25 GPU-hours). Cost 38.6 GB RSS per graph lane (8,541 unique targets x ~4.5 MB; measured 38.1 GB warm vs 38.6 predicted), ~116 GB of 440 GB free. GPU util median 46% -> 99%/84%/43%. SAMPLER THREAD CLIFF (2026-07-17): sample_subgraph costs 28.3 ms/target at 1 thread, 19.9 at 8, and 470.7 at this box's 64-thread default -- 17x slower. run_screening/test_screening already pin set_num_threads(1); any caller that forgets inherits the cliff. REGISTRY LOCK: register_run/log_run now hold an exclusive advisory flock (its own ponytail marker said to add it before concurrent GPU lanes). Unlocked is worse than lossy -- write_text_atomic stages through a FIXED <name>.tmp, so concurrent lanes collide and the loser's rename raises FileNotFoundError, i.e. the campaign would have crashed partway. Verified: 4 truly concurrent lanes -> 4 runs, 4 distinct ids, zero loss. CLI: --only NAME (one config per process; writes its row + predictions + registry entry but NOT summary.json, which would claim the whole wave), --merge (recombine -> summary.json + H2a/H2b; a lane that never landed is recorded 'missing' and exits non-zero rather than silently shortening the wave), --promote (screening/promotion.py: nothing existed to name the frozen H1 that feat-010/012/013 all consume -- N_FINAL_SEEDS was declared in config and referenced nowhere). Promotion ranks on systema, NOT best_val (not comparable across the family: typed_static val 490.4 vs expression_only 3.47 on this run, because the graph members' totals carry regulariser terms expression_only has no equivalent of); refuses a row with no checkpoint on disk; excludes the non-neural network_propagation reference; breaks exact ties by name; flags a first-vs-second margin inside --noise-margin as a coin toss. It is the SINGLE-SEED screening promotion, not the report's 5-seed promotion (a separate campaign). EPOCH BUDGET: 20, and the budget IS the wall clock -- Trainer early-stops on val total with patience=10 but a min-delta of 1e-6 against ~1e-3/epoch improvements, so it will not fire (measured on this run: untyped_gnn val -0.0047 over 5 epochs). If val is still descending at epoch 20 the verdict is 'not converged at budget', not convergence. TESTS: ./init.sh green at 268 (252 + 16), every one watched failing first; 10/10 mutations caught. Mutation testing caught TWO of the new tests passing for the wrong reason: the edges-invalidation test appended to edge_index AND edge_attr so the edge_attr shape change masked a missing topology stamp (fixed with an in-place rewire test), and the isolation test mutated the miss path's return value rather than a cache HIT so dropping .clone() stayed green (fixed). Also verified rather than assumed: HeteroData.to(device) MUTATES IN PLACE and returns the same object, so serving cached objects would migrate the 38 GB host cache onto the GPU -- hits are cloned; and a subgraph depends on strictly more than the CSR index does (it embeds x[sel] and edge_attr[kept]), hence _content_fingerprint over topology + x + edge_attr + complex count. The sealed challenge split was NOT touched. STALE-PARQUET GUARD (2026-07-17, added while the campaign ran): --merge/--promote read <screening_root>/<name>/<seed>.parquet, and the tree accumulates parquets across runs -- confirmed live: condition_gated/typed_static parquets were STALE (15:44/15:49, a prior run) while the campaign's own lanes were still training, so a manual --merge would have reported last run's numbers as this result (stale-wrong, worse than missing). Fixed: merge_lane_results + promote take an optional registry_path; a config whose LATEST registry run (per seed) is not 'completed' is forced to status=missing / skipped even if a parquet exists (log_run flips status in place, so newest run per config_id is the truth). CRUCIAL EDGE CASE: network_propagation is never registered (non-neural reference, not a capped trial), so the guard must only DEMOTE tracked-but-incomplete configs and fall back to parquet presence for UNTRACKED ones -- else the registry guard silently drops the netprop row. Mutation testing caught this: the over-rejecting variant (latest.get(name)!=completed) passed until a test pinning 'untracked config with a fresh parquet is kept' was added. Guard mutations now caught 2/2; ./init.sh green at 271. LIVE gpu_hours so far: untyped_gnn 2.31 (est 2.3), expression_only 0.359 (est 0.35) -- both dead-on. condition_gated overfits val after epoch 1 (best@ep1), so its promoted checkpoint is early; the harness scores the BEST checkpoint, so this is handled, not a bug. CAMPAIGN COMPLETE (2026-07-18 03:32 IST, 8.2 h wall / 17.7 GPU-hours over 3 A100s) -- NEGATIVE RESULT, reported not tuned. All 5 members completed on the shared full fold (21,262 train / 4,400 val). systema ranking: untyped_gnn 0.0951 > expression_only 0.0861 > condition_gated 0.0834 > typed_static 0.0786 > network_propagation 0.0319. H2a (typed_static > expression_only) NOT supported, Dsystema=-0.0075 -- the typed PPI graph LOWERS systema below no-graph. H2b (condition_gated > typed_static) supported Dsystema=+0.0048, BUT condition_gated (0.0834) is still below expression_only (0.0861), so the full typed+gated EG-IPG does NOT beat the no-graph baseline; H2b is a within-family recovery, not evidence the graph earns its place. The untyped-GCN diagnostic is the best model -- exactly the 'untyped-graph diagnostic' outcome the report anticipated. All neural members within ~0.017 systema on a 0.086 base; single-seed screening so NO error bars (the report's 5-seed promotion is a separate step). CONVERGENCE ASYMMETRY: expression_only ran the full 20 epochs STILL DESCENDING (best@ep19) -- its 0.0861 is not-converged and would likely climb; the typed graph models overfit immediately (typed_static best@ep2 early-stopped@13, condition_gated best@ep1 early-stopped@12), so more budget widens the gap AGAINST the graph. (My pre-launch claim that early stopping would not fire was WRONG -- it fired on both typed lanes because they overfit, not because val plateaued slowly.) Registry gpu_hours: condition_gated 8.17, typed_static 6.89, untyped_gnn 2.31, expression_only 0.359. GPU util 79-99% on graph lanes (cache moved the bottleneck off CPU sampling). PROMOTION (promoted.json): mechanical rank-on-systema picks untyped_gnn FINAL / expression_only runner-up, margin +0.0090 flagged WITHIN NOISE (0.01 band). This pick is scientifically awkward and is left to the PI: untyped_gnn has no gates/typing so feat-012's rationale audit CANNOT run on it, and the pre-registered confirmatory H1 (condition_gated) ranked 3rd of 4 below no-graph. Options (none a tuning move): freeze untyped_gnn (feat-012 N/A) / freeze condition_gated (underperformed) / promote nothing (null fold). feat-010/012/013 blocked on this choice. The sealed split (5,608 rows) remains UNOPENED. ./init.sh green at 271. H1 FROZEN (2026-07-18, PI decision): condition_gated, the pre-registered confirmatory H1, via --promote --pin condition_gated -- NOT the argmax screening winner (untyped_gnn). Rationale: a confirmatory protocol commits to the typed+gated H1 before the fold, so keeping it (not swapping in the post-hoc winner) is what makes the negative result honest, and it is the ONLY choice under which feat-012's rationale audit can run (untyped_gnn has no gates/typing to audit). promoted.json records it without spin: final=condition_gated, pinned_rank 3/4, screening_winner=untyped_gnn, runner_up=untyped_gnn, margin=-0.0117 (the frozen H1 is BEHIND the model that won screening), margin_within_noise=False. Frozen checkpoint data/results/screening/condition_gated/0/ckpt/stage_a_best.pt is what feat-010/012/013 load. IMPLEMENTATION: promote() gained a pin= param (freeze a specific config as final regardless of rank, recording pinned/pinned_rank/screening_winner honestly; raises if the pinned config has no completed result -- no silent fallback to the winner) + margin_within_noise now uses abs() so a pinned H1 behind its runner-up is still judged by gap SIZE not sign; driver --pin NAME. Tests watched failing then green; pin logic mutation-tested 3/3 (pin-ignored, missing-pin-guard-removed, pinned_rank-hardwired). ./init.sh green at 273. feat-010/012/013 now unblocked (each a separate campaign, not started). NOTE for feat-012: run_module8_real.py run_audit still has a STALE message 'the graph model cannot converge until the mini-batch refactor lands' -- false now, correct it. Sealed split (5,608 rows) remains UNOPENED. XHIGH /code-review OF b875dfa (2026-07-18): 6 findings (2 CONFIRMED, 4 PLAUSIBLE), all reproduced then fixed test-first + mutation-tested. (1) CONFIRMED promote() crashed on a non-finite systema (json.dumps allow_nan=False) -- filter non-finite from ranking + _finite_or_none the dump. (2) CONFIRMED freshness guard dropped a completed-then-failed lane (keyed on 'latest != completed') -- now _config_statuses + _is_stale: fresh iff 'completed' in statuses AND latest != 'registered' (distinguishes a re-reservation from a crashed re-run); pinned by the finding test AND its dual. (4) cache key omitted gene_to_idx -> key on the RESOLVED seed. (2-ABA) data_ptr fingerprint could ABA-collide -> _TensorSet holds tensor OBJECTS, compares by is + _version (a held ref prevents address reuse), applied to BOTH the subgraph cache and the CSR index; equivalence tests confirm sampling stays bit-identical. (5) promote_final hard-coded 'systema' -> PRIMARY_METRIC constant. (3) --noise-margin 0.0 footgun -> loud note when no band is set. Built src/tcell_pipeline/screening/multiseed.py: paired_delta_summary() runs a one-sample (paired) t on the per-seed deltas d_s = systema(better,s) - systema(worse,s) against 0 (mean/sd/se/t/p and a 95% CI via scipy t_{n-1}); a seed missing from either arm or non-finite in either is DROPPED (named in `dropped`) and shrinks n; n<2 gives a mean but no CI; zero-variance deltas give a zero-width CI with no division; the verdict is honest ('indistinguishable ... the paired CI crosses zero' is NOT support for the better arm). aggregate_seeds() reads each (config,seed) parquet through the SAME freshness fence as merge/promote (_config_statuses/_is_stale) plus a registry split check (single_frozen_fold). CORRECTION (2026-07-20, xhigh workflow /code-review of f1a00dd -- 15 verified findings, 2 of them claim-level). The 5-seed NUMBERS above are UNCHANGED and the paired-t math was verified CORRECT (every 'the statistic is wrong' candidate was REFUTED); what was wrong were two CONCLUSIONS drawn from them. The block above is left intact per append-only; this supersedes it. (1) 'the frozen H1 condition_gated (0.0838) still sits BELOW no-graph expression_only (0.0857)' was read off two marginal per-config means -- that pair was NOT in CONTRASTS and was never tested. Run as a proper paired contrast it is condition_gated - expression_only = -0.0019, 95% CI [-0.0042,+0.0004], p=0.0847: the CI CROSSES ZERO. The honest statement is that the frozen H1 is at statistical PARITY with no-graph on this fold -- it does NOT beat no-graph, and it cannot be said to sit BELOW it either. h1_vs_no_graph is now a first-class entry in CONTRASTS so the claim can never again be read off marginal means. (2) The contrasts were each tested at raw alpha=0.05 with NO multiplicity control. With family-wise correction over the (now four) simultaneous contrasts: h2a p=0.0036 -> Bonferroni 0.0142 SURVIVES; h2b p=0.0092 -> 0.0369 SURVIVES; promotion_margin p=0.0208 -> 0.0832 DOES NOT SURVIVE (Holm 0.0416; survives_family_wise requires BOTH methods, the conservative call, so the correction method cannot be shopped after seeing which one rescues a claim); h1_vs_no_graph 0.0847 -> 0.3389. The claim 'the ONLY graph variant that reliably beats no-graph is the plain UNTYPED gnn (+0.0045)' is therefore RETRACTED: nominally positive, NOT robust to multiplicity. CORRECTED BOTTOM LINE: after multiplicity control NO graph variant reliably beats no-graph; the typed variant is reliably WORSE (h2a, survives correction); the frozen H1 is at PARITY; h2b only means gating REPAIRS the damage typing did. The core negative for the EG-IPG premise STANDS and is cleaner than first published -- the error was claiming more resolution than the data supports, in BOTH directions. 13 further guard defects fixed, all red-first with constructed breakers: the fold gate now keys on recorded n_train/n_val (screen_config now writes them) because the registry `split` label is filled from cfg.get('split','blocked_target_ood') while nested_family_configs never sets it -- a hardcoded literal that can only CONFIRM, never refute, so a --n-max capped seed passed the old gate;
