features.name: Predictive-Rationale Audit
features.description: Test necessity, sufficiency, minimality, and stability of returned subgraphs against matched random controls; run structural-OOD diagnostics
features.evidence: IN-PROGRESS (2026-07-17, Module 8 — Predictive-Rationale Audit; report §Module 4 audit). Audit machinery DONE + tested; running it on the FROZEN H1 model (a converged graph model, blocked on the Module-7 mini-batch refactor) is the remaining compute. src/tcell_pipeline/rationale/rationale_audit.py: audit_rationale(model, head, dataset, n_cases=50) runs on the frozen H1 model + fitted rationale head (NO training) over a stratified case set (degree x effect-size x condition x graph-coverage, round-robin so the subset spans buckets; uncovered targets filtered out BEFORE selection so they don't burn the case budget). Per covered case it REUSES the Module-4 machinery unchanged — RationaleHead (extract S), FaithfulnessTester (fixed-model sufficiency/necessity + structural_ood_audit), MatchedRandomSampler (size+relation-matched controls) — and adds: a minimality curve (sufficiency as top-ranked edges are added back + the minimal prefix fraction), per-source ablation (delta prediction when BioPlex/HuRI/STRING/CORUM edges are removed via a keep-mask over PP AND the 100%-CORUM complex_membership edges), a GInX-style rationale-vs-random-at-sparsity comparison, and stability (Jaccard of selected edges across DropEdge-on re-encodes). Aggregates: frac sufficiency<random, frac necessity>random, mean minimality, mean stability, per-source mean delta, GInX curves -> <RATIONALE_AUDIT_ROOT>/audit_report.json. Requires a graph model (expression-only -> ValueError). config: RATIONALE_AUDIT_ROOT, N_RATIONALE_AUDIT_CASES=50. Tests: src/tests/test_rationale_audit.py (3) — end-to-end audit produces per-case + aggregate diagnostics and writes the report; CORUM ablation reaches the complex_membership edges; expression-only model rejected. Adversarial review (2026-07-17): 2 mediums fixed (CORUM ablation now includes membership edges; uncovered targets no longer consume audit slots). ./init.sh green at 200 tests. Spec docs/specs/2026-07-17-module8-comparators-audit-sealed-repro.md. REMAINING for done: run the audit on the promoted frozen H1 (needs a converged graph model) + the full 50-case real-data campaign. · xhigh /code-review pass 2 (2026-07-17, 63 agents): 2 confirmed findings FIXED. (1) DEVICE — audit_rationale moved model to device but never the head, and _audit_one's device param was dead, so a cuda node-state into the head's CPU nn.Linear killed the audit on the first case (device= was advertised but unusable). FIXED: head.to(device); the dead param became the seed. Fixing it let the audit reach a GPU for the first time and immediately surfaced a LATENT MODULE-4 BUG: RationaleHead._select builds rel_id/local on CPU then indexes them with idx from a CUDA topk -> RuntimeError; the head had never run on GPU (its tests are CPU-only). FIXED with idx=idx.cpu(). (2) REPRODUCIBILITY — _stability enables DropEdge, which draws from the GLOBAL torch RNG that the audit seed never touched, so mean_stability in audit_report.json was not reproducible from (model, head, dataset, seed) (reviewers measured 1.0/0.778/0.852 at one audit seed). FIXED: the global RNG is seeded from the audit seed and its prior state restored. +2 regression tests (a real CUDA audit run; stability identical across ambient RNG states). ./init.sh green at 215 tests. · PASS 3 — adversarial verification OF the pass-2 fixes (2026-07-17, 17 agents): the device fix RESOLVED (a real CUDA audit completes), but _stability's fix was PARTIAL — torch.manual_seed reseeds the CPU AND every CUDA generator while torch.random.get/set_rng_state cover the CPU generator only, so the docstring's 'nothing leaks to the caller' was false and the caller's CUDA RNG was clobbered. FIXED: torch.cuda.get/set_rng_state_all saved+restored when CUDA is available. ./init.sh green at 224 tests. THROUGHPUT REFACTOR (2026-07-17, graph mini-batch goal): the compute ceiling that blocked this feature's real-data campaign is LIFTED. Profiling the real 25,440-node graph on an A100 showed the deferred task's premise was wrong: message passing was only 5% of wall-clock (34 ms/row) while sample_subgraph was 95% (581 ms/row), because _grow/_induce scanned the whole ~8M-edge table PER ROW (torch.isin alone = 59% of sampler time, _induce's remap gather 28%, argsort 0.03%). Fixed at root cause in order: (1) neighborhood_sampler._NeighborIndex -- a per-relation CSR built ONCE per graph (0.85 s, ~130 MB), so incident-edge lookup is O(sum degree) not O(|E|): 581 -> 26 ms/row; (2) then PyG Batch.from_data_list mini-batching of the now-dominant message passing in TypedGraphEncoder.forward + UntypedGraphEncoder.forward (condition gate scattered per edge via the batch vector so each edge is gated by ITS OWN sample's condition; readout uses to_dense_batch + key_padding_mask so attention cannot leak across targets and still sums to 1 per sample; gates/confidences split back to the per-sample list contract Module 4 and StageALoss._graph consume). MEASURED on an A100, real graph, forward+backward, bs=8: 667 -> 71 ms/row (9.4x); 21,262-row epoch 3.94 h -> 0.42 h; GPU utilisation median 1% -> 43% (p90 86%). bs=8 is the sweet spot (bs=32 = 15.2 vs 14.0 rows/s for 31.5 vs 10.0 GB). CORRECTNESS: exact-equivalence tests pin both halves -- the sampler is bit-identical to the frozen full-scan oracle (real-graph subgraphs unchanged: min 465 / max 512 nodes, mean 30,751 PP edges) and batched forward matches the per-sample loop edge-for-edge on CPU AND CUDA; oracles are self-contained (importing the sampler's policy constants would move both sides and silently void the test -- caught and fixed during this work); teeth verified by injecting 6 defects, each caught. DropEdge is train-only/random so equivalence is asserted in eval(). NOT saturated and the reason is known: the batch is still sampled row-by-row on CPU (~26 ms/row, ~37% of a step) -- next ceiling is batch-aware sampling / per-target subgraph cache / DataLoader workers (deliberately not done, YAGNI). ./init.sh green at 236 (224 + 12). Real-data smoke, 600 rows / 1 epoch, machinery only: untyped_gnn systema=0.0534, typed_static systema=0.0531, condition_gated systema=0.0432. The campaign itself remains UNRUN, so this feature stays in-progress. REVIEW (2026-07-17, xhigh /code-review of ef8c1ad, 37 agents -> 35 verified -> 15 distinct defects, ALL FIXED; full record in docs/specs/2026-07-17-graph-throughput-minibatch.md). The refactor was functionally correct as shipped, and the review found what the equivalence tests could not see. (1) REAL REGRESSION: h_graph was preallocated torch.zeros(...) = float32, where the torch.stack it replaced inherited the readout's dtype -- under torch.autocast the bf16 readout was cast back up into an fp32 h_graph, so the graph pathway left the autocast region at a different dtype from every other pathway; now allocated with pooled.dtype, and the all-absent early return (no readout runs) mirrors h_do.dtype, the tensor ProgramDecoder concatenates it with. (2) TEST BLINDNESS, the important one: _grow's order-restoring torch.sort is the single line the bit-identity claim rests on and it READS redundant, but _PRIORITY_BONUS adds 1e6 to a float32 score (spacing 0.0625 at 1e6) so scores quantise into ties in bulk and the tie-break order decides which neighbours survive the cap; deleting it left all 237 tests GREEN while the sampler diverged from the oracle on 36/600 (gene,cap,hops) combos and 35 of the fixture's 60 genes -- the four hand-picked probe genes were exactly the ones that cannot fail, and the module docstring asserted the OPPOSITE of incident()'s real contract, inviting the deletion. Fixed by sweeping EVERY fixture gene (the suite now catches that mutation), correcting the docstring, and labelling both sorts load-bearing. (3) VACUOUS TEST: allclose(g, ones_like(g)) is True on a zero-length gate, so a forward returning empty gates for every sample passed green -- which would zero StageALoss._graph's sparsity+unsourced terms and empty the Module-4 rationale with H2b still reported; now pinned to the sub-graph's real edge counts. (4) CACHE STALENESS: the CSR index was cached on the graph with no invalidation, so an appended edge was silently never seen (the full scan it replaced re-read edge_index every call) -- a rewired-network or edge-ablation control would have been cut from the pre-edit topology; fixed with a (data_ptr, shape, _version) fingerprint that rebuilds on change. (5) torch.split handed out VIEWS into the batch-wide gate tensor where the per-sample loop gave independent storage -> .clone(). (6) positional indexing narrowed input from any iterable to a sequence -> list(). (7) _gather's torch.arange ignored the graph's device (latent). Hot-path waste removed (ea[eids, col] instead of materialising every column; _induce's always-true src conjunct; 4n empty tensors -> one shared blank) took sampling 26 -> 22 ms/row and the end-to-end figure from 9.4x to 10.9x: 667 -> 61 ms/row, epoch 3.94 h -> 0.36 h, GPU util median 1% -> 46% (p90 94%). Every fix is red-green verified by reverting it and watching the matching test fail. ./init.sh green at 242. Real-graph subgraph shapes re-verified UNCHANGED (min 465 / max 512 nodes, mean 30,751 PP edges). LESSON: hand-picked probe cases are a way of choosing what the test cannot see -- mutate the one line the central claim rests on, not just the ones you thought of. REVIEW FOLLOW-UP (2026-07-17): the 2 findings first waved through are now fixed too, so all 15 are resolved. (a) incident()'s duplicate-free precondition was docstring-only -> now enforced (a repeated node re-emits its whole CSR row, so its edges return once per repeat and the sub-graph silently carries duplicates the boolean scan never produced; the check is <=512 nodes against a ~160k-edge gather, i.e. free). (b) EVAL PEAK MEMORY was a real regression, not inherent as first argued: the per-row loop bounded eval peak to ONE subgraph REGARDLESS of batch size, and screening.collect_predictions defaults to batch_size=config.BATCH_SIZE=64 under no_grad while the typed encoder OOMs 80GB at batch 32 on real dense subgraphs -- and the original goal had explicitly asked for 'a working batch size / chunking path' that was never built. Both encoders now message-pass at most config.GRAPH_ENCODE_CHUNK (default 8, env- overridable) subgraphs at once and stitch the results, so a caller's batch_size sets the OPTIMISATION batch, not the memory ceiling. MEASURED at batch 64 under no_grad on the real graph: peak 12.53 GB -> 2.01 GB (6.2x) and slightly FASTER (2.9s -> 2.5s). Chunking is invisible to results: every chunk size (1/2/3/8/off) agrees with the unchunked batch edge-for-edge (test_encode_chunking_does_not_change_results) and test_chunking_bounds_message_passing_width asserts no more than `chunk` subgraphs are ever in flight; teeth verified by injecting 4 defects (never-chunk, reversed stitch, mis-assigned gates, dropped guard), each caught. HONEST LIMIT: chunking does NOT reduce TRAINING memory -- autograd retains every chunk's activations until backward, so a batch-64 training step still peaks at 55.9 GB; batch size remains the training knob and chunking bounds evaluation. Steady-state sampling unchanged at 22 ms/row (the new guard costs nothing measurable); ./init.sh green at 252.

features.evidence: IN-PROGRESS (2026-07-17, Module 8 — Reproducibility Verification + the sealed challenge evaluator; report §reproducibility + §Phase 5). Verifier + 11/11 fallacy scan + sealed evaluator DONE + tested; an actual clean-checkout reproduction of a real frozen run is the remaining compute. src/tcell_pipeline/reproducibility/: verify.py — verify_reproducibility(checkout, manifest) re-derives each frozen item against a clean checkout and returns one of REPRODUCIBLE / PARTIALLY_REPRODUCIBLE / NOT_REPRODUCIBLE / CANNOT_VERIFY: deterministic-hash reproduction (id_mapping/splits/de_layers, streamed sha256, CRITICAL), prediction schema + row counts, config hash, checkpoint provenance, the confirmatory decision (observed vs frozen within tolerance, CRITICAL), and the 11/11 fallacy scan (CRITICAL). (B) DECISION still vacuous: pass 2 added PRESENCE guards but never touched the bool() coercion that WAS the defect — bool(None)==bool(None), bool('false') is True. FIXED: h1_confirmed must be a real bool in both records, numeric fields real numbers. (C) ROOT CAUSE pass 2 missed: _corr returned a 0.0 SENTINEL for three distinct degeneracies (<2 pairs, x constant, y constant), indistinguishable from a genuine zero — exactly how berkson false-flagged; pass 2's row-count guard was re-triggered with a constant x among >=3 selected rows. FIXED: _corr raises Unevaluable when undefined, closing the false-flag path in berkson/collider/ecological/simpson at once. (D) regression_to_mean was shift-invariant but NOT scale-invariant (f=b*0.5 still flagged at correlation 1.0) -> deviations now measured in each series' OWN standard-deviation units (blind to any affine change; the pass-2 flagging fixture was itself an affine followup and had to be replaced with a genuine regression case). (E) reverse_causation's new min_forward=0.1 floor introduced a FALSE NEGATIVE on the archetypal trap (weak forward f=0.05 dominated by reverse r=0.95 went unflagged) -> the floor now applies to the STRONGER direction (max(f,r)>=min_association); only the null endpoint is excluded. (F) the silent-clean-pass CLASS was only patched at survivorship — base_rate (empty/single-class), collider (constant z, 1 row), simpson (single group), look_elsewhere (empty p-values), garden_of_forks (single estimate), correlation_not_causation/reverse_causation (NaN) all still certified clean on undefined input -> EVERY detector now validates its input. (G) NaN no longer crashed but still produced WRONG flags (collider |partial|>1 fired; look_elsewhere/garden_of_forks flagged on NaN) -> finite validation throughout. (H) _verdict was BLACKLIST-shaped so any novel status certified -> now whitelist (a critical check certifies only on an explicit 'pass'). (I) _check_predictions had no required-block guard (fix #2's own class at the untouched sibling: no predictions block -> zero checks -> REPRODUCIBLE) -> an absent block is now a missing check. (J) the manifest could set its own bar (tolerance 1e9/inf waved through any drift incl a sign flip) -> capped at MAX_DECISION_TOLERANCE=0.01. (K) malformed manifests RAISED instead of returning a verdict (contract violation, no report written) -> every entry validated -> missing -> CANNOT_VERIFY. (L) _resolve over-rejected: .resolve() follows symlinks so an in-checkout artifact symlinked to a shared store (this project's documented env-overridable data layout) was falsely rejected -> containment is now LEXICAL (normpath), still blocking absolute paths and '..'. (M) the H1 note was an unconditional string literal emitted even when rho_perturbed_mean was demonstrably non-zero, so the sealed JSON contradicted itself -> the note is derived from the computed value with a WARNING variant + a perturbed_mean_is_structural_zero flag. Two bugs in my own pass-3 work were caught by red-green: _safe_split_label rejected EVERY label ('(os.altsep or "") in s' is '"" in s' on POSIX -> always True), and test_verify_cannot_verify_vacuous_decision passed with the bool coercion reverted (it only exercised the presence guard) -> replaced with a test that fails without the fix. +9 regression tests, all red-green verified; every original reviewer attack replayed against the fixed code and confirmed closed. ./init.sh green at 224 tests. THROUGHPUT REFACTOR (2026-07-17, graph mini-batch goal): the compute ceiling that blocked this feature's real-data campaign is LIFTED. Profiling the real 25,440-node graph on an A100 showed the deferred task's premise was wrong: message passing was only 5% of wall-clock (34 ms/row) while sample_subgraph was 95% (581 ms/row), because _grow/_induce scanned the whole ~8M-edge table PER ROW (torch.isin alone = 59% of sampler time, _induce's remap gather 28%, argsort 0.03%). Fixed at root cause in order: (1) neighborhood_sampler._NeighborIndex -- a per-relation CSR built ONCE per graph (0.85 s, ~130 MB), so incident-edge lookup is O(sum degree) not O(|E|): 581 -> 26 ms/row; (2) then PyG Batch.from_data_list mini-batching of the now-dominant message passing in TypedGraphEncoder.forward + UntypedGraphEncoder.forward (condition gate scattered per edge via the batch vector so each edge is gated by ITS OWN sample's condition; readout uses to_dense_batch + key_padding_mask so attention cannot leak across targets and still sums to 1 per sample; gates/confidences split back to the per-sample list contract Module 4 and StageALoss._graph consume). MEASURED on an A100, real graph, forward+backward, bs=8: 667 -> 71 ms/row (9.4x); 21,262-row epoch 3.94 h -> 0.42 h; GPU utilisation median 1% -> 43% (p90 86%). bs=8 is the sweet spot (bs=32 = 15.2 vs 14.0 rows/s for 31.5 vs 10.0 GB). CORRECTNESS: exact-equivalence tests pin both halves -- the sampler is bit-identical to the frozen full-scan oracle (real-graph subgraphs unchanged: min 465 / max 512 nodes, mean 30,751 PP edges) and batched forward matches the per-sample loop edge-for-edge on CPU AND CUDA; oracles are self-contained (importing the sampler's policy constants would move both sides and silently void the test -- caught and fixed during this work); teeth verified by injecting 6 defects, each caught. DropEdge is train-only/random so equivalence is asserted in eval(). NOT saturated and the reason is known: the batch is still sampled row-by-row on CPU (~26 ms/row, ~37% of a step) -- next ceiling is batch-aware sampling / per-target subgraph cache / DataLoader workers (deliberately not done, YAGNI). ./init.sh green at 236 (224 + 12). Real-data smoke, 600 rows / 1 epoch, machinery only: untyped_gnn systema=0.0534, typed_static systema=0.0531, condition_gated systema=0.0432. The campaign itself remains UNRUN, so this feature stays in-progress. REVIEW (2026-07-17, xhigh /code-review of ef8c1ad, 37 agents -> 35 verified -> 15 distinct defects, ALL FIXED; full record in docs/specs/2026-07-17-graph-throughput-minibatch.md). The refactor was functionally correct as shipped, and the review found what the equivalence tests could not see. (1) REAL REGRESSION: h_graph was preallocated torch.zeros(...) = float32, where the torch.stack it replaced inherited the readout's dtype -- under torch.autocast the bf16 readout was cast back up into an fp32 h_graph, so the graph pathway left the autocast region at a different dtype from every other pathway; now allocated with pooled.dtype, and the all-absent early return (no readout runs) mirrors h_do.dtype, the tensor ProgramDecoder concatenates it with. (2) TEST BLINDNESS, the important one: _grow's order-restoring torch.sort is the single line the bit-identity claim rests on and it READS redundant, but _PRIORITY_BONUS adds 1e6 to a float32 score (spacing 0.0625 at 1e6) so scores quantise into ties in bulk and the tie-break order decides which neighbours survive the cap; deleting it left all 237 tests GREEN while the sampler diverged from the oracle on 36/600 (gene,cap,hops) combos and 35 of the fixture's 60 genes -- the four hand-picked probe genes were exactly the ones that cannot fail, and the module docstring asserted the OPPOSITE of incident()'s real contract, inviting the deletion. Fixed by sweeping EVERY fixture gene (the suite now catches that mutation), correcting the docstring, and labelling both sorts load-bearing. (3) VACUOUS TEST: allclose(g, ones_like(g)) is True on a zero-length gate, so a forward returning empty gates for every sample passed green -- which would zero StageALoss._graph's sparsity+unsourced terms and empty the Module-4 rationale with H2b still reported; now pinned to the sub-graph's real edge counts. (4) CACHE STALENESS: the CSR index was cached on the graph with no invalidation, so an appended edge was silently never seen (the full scan it replaced re-read edge_index every call) -- a rewired-network or edge-ablation control would have been cut from the pre-edit topology; fixed with a (data_ptr, shape, _version) fingerprint that rebuilds on change. (5) torch.split handed out VIEWS into the batch-wide gate tensor where the per-sample loop gave independent storage -> .clone(). (6) positional indexing narrowed input from any iterable to a sequence -> list(). (7) _gather's torch.arange ignored the graph's device (latent). Hot-path waste removed (ea[eids, col] instead of materialising every column; _induce's always-true src conjunct; 4n empty tensors -> one shared blank) took sampling 26 -> 22 ms/row and the end-to-end figure from 9.4x to 10.9x: 667 -> 61 ms/row, epoch 3.94 h -> 0.36 h, GPU util median 1% -> 46% (p90 94%). Every fix is red-green verified by reverting it and watching the matching test fail. ./init.sh green at 242. Real-graph subgraph shapes re-verified UNCHANGED (min 465 / max 512 nodes, mean 30,751 PP edges). LESSON: hand-picked probe cases are a way of choosing what the test cannot see -- mutate the one line the central claim rests on, not just the ones you thought of. REVIEW FOLLOW-UP (2026-07-17): the 2 findings first waved through are now fixed too, so all 15 are resolved. (a) incident()'s duplicate-free precondition was docstring-only -> now enforced (a repeated node re-emits its whole CSR row, so its edges return once per repeat and the sub-graph silently carries duplicates the boolean scan never produced; the check is <=512 nodes against a ~160k-edge gather, i.e. free). (b) EVAL PEAK MEMORY was a real regression, not inherent as first argued: the per-row loop bounded eval peak to ONE subgraph REGARDLESS of batch size, and screening.collect_predictions defaults to batch_size=config.BATCH_SIZE=64 under no_grad while the typed encoder OOMs 80GB at batch 32 on real dense subgraphs -- and the original goal had explicitly asked for 'a working batch size / chunking path' that was never built. Both encoders now message-pass at most config.GRAPH_ENCODE_CHUNK (default 8, env- overridable) subgraphs at once and stitch the results, so a caller's batch_size sets the OPTIMISATION batch, not the memory ceiling. MEASURED at batch 64 under no_grad on the real graph: peak 12.53 GB -> 2.01 GB (6.2x) and slightly FASTER (2.9s -> 2.5s). Chunking is invisible to results: every chunk size (1/2/3/8/off) agrees with the unchunked batch edge-for-edge (test_encode_chunking_does_not_change_results) and test_chunking_bounds_message_passing_width asserts no more than `chunk` subgraphs are ever in flight; teeth verified by injecting 4 defects (never-chunk, reversed stitch, mis-assigned gates, dropped guard), each caught. HONEST LIMIT: chunking does NOT reduce TRAINING memory -- autograd retains every chunk's activations until backward, so a batch-64 training step still peaks at 55.9 GB; batch size remains the training knob and chunking bounds evaluation. Steady-state sampling unchanged at 22 ms/row (the new guard costs nothing measurable); ./init.sh green at 252.

features.name: Perturbation & Context Encoder (Module 1)
features.description: Encode each (perturbation, context) into h_do in R^256 from q_pre features only: frozen PLM+PINNACLE target embeddings + PPI degree + control baseline, trainable culture-condition embedding, donor PCs via Linear (no free donor-ID embedding), guide-quality features; leakage fence rejects q_post at the module boundary. Component of feat-008, built ahead since it depends only on Module 0 outputs.
features.evidence: src/tcell_pipeline/encoders/ package. PluggableEmbeddingStore: frozen PLM (1280) / PINNACLE (512) lookup by UniProt accession, in-memory cache, zero-vector fallback when the parquet is absent (embeddings not computed yet), validates embedding dim; NOT an nn.Module. TargetEncoder: NO trainable gene-ID embedding (H1 prohibited); h_target in R^1796 = PLM 1280 + PINNACLE 512 + ppi_degree_physical/functional/complex + control_baseline_expr. ContextEncoder: trainable nn.Embedding(3,64) for Rest/Stim8hr/Stim48hr + donor_pc_00..31 through Linear(32,32) (NO free donor-ID embedding, so leave-one-donor-out stays valid); h_context in R^96. QualityEncoder: n_guides + single_guide_estimate + zeros(64) guide-seq placeholder; h_quality in R^66. PerturbationEncoder: forward(batch_dict) -> (B,256); fusion Linear(1958->256) + LayerNorm; rejects any q_post column at the boundary (ValueError). CPU-only, batch-first, 503,264 trainable params. config: added PLM_EMBED_DIM/PINNACLE_EMBED_DIM/GUIDE_SEQ_EMBED_DIM/H_DO_DIM/CONDITIONS/PLM_EMBEDDINGS_PATH/PINNACLE_EMBEDDINGS_PATH. NaN guard: as_float_vector nan_to_num neutralises missing control_baseline_expr (1558/33983 rows) and n_guides so one NaN cannot poison the LayerNorm'd h_do (ponytail: upgrade to fold-fit imputation in the Module 3 loader). Verified: 10 tests in src/tests/test_encoders.py (zero-fallback, dims, distinct condition embeddings, no-NaN batch, only the 3-way condition Embedding exists, q_post rejected, missing-scalar guard) + real-data smoke feeding perturbation_condition/de_obs head through the encoder -> finite (4,256) with PLM/PINNACLE absent. init.sh green (compileall + 33 pytest).

Corrects the PINNACLE dim placeholder.

Sequences fetched from UniProt REST accessions endpoint (cached to uniprot_sequences.parquet); resumable (skips embedded proteins, atomic checkpoint) writing config.PLM_EMBEDDINGS_PATH. features.name: Typed Graph Encoder (Module 2)
features.description: Condition-gated signed relational GNN over the typed PPI graph: build a PyG HeteroData (protein + complex nodes; physical_ppi/co_complex/functional_assoc/complex_membership relations), sample a bounded neighbourhood per perturbation target, run 3-layer message passing where messages are signed (tanh*relu) and gated per condition, and cross-attend h_do over the node states to produce h_graph in R^256 plus per-relation edge gates for Module 4. Component of feat-008, built ahead since it depends only on Module 0 outputs + Module 1's h_do.
features.evidence: src/tcell_pipeline/graph/ package (torch_geometric 2.8). graph_builder.build_hetero_graph -> (HeteroData, gene_to_idx): protein nodes keyed by upper-case HGNC (25440 on real data) carrying the same frozen 1412-d descriptor as Module 1's TargetEncoder (PLM 1280 + PINNACLE 128 + 3 graph-derived degrees + control_baseline_expr, zero-fallback via PluggableEmbeddingStore); complex nodes (5628, index-only, learned nn.Embedding in the encoder); 4 relations split by is_physical/is_complex/is_functional flags + bipartite complex_membership, each with an 8-d edge feature (source one-hot(5)|score|is_direct_binary|n_supporting). Real edge counts: physical_ppi 1123205, co_complex 48389, functional_assoc 6857702, complex_membership 18932. neighborhood_sampler.sample_subgraph: grows physical/co-complex first then fills by score, caps at NEIGHBORHOOD_CAP=512 protein nodes, pulls in member complexes, returns an induced HeteroData preserving orig_idx. typed_graph_encoder.TypedGraphEncoder(nn.Module): projects protein features + complex embeddings to 256-d, 3 GRAPH_LAYERS of per-relation custom MessagePassing (W_r/U_r/W_sign/W_mag) with signed message m=tanh(W_sign h_u)*relu(W_mag h_u) and residual FFN+LayerNorm per node type; condition gate alpha=sigmoid(w_gate[h_cond(64)||f_e(8)]) computed once per relation (layer-independent) and returned as edge_gates for Module 4 (the SAME edge is gated differently per culture condition); DropEdge p=0.1 in training. graph_readout.GraphReadout: 4-head cross-attention (q=h_do, K=V=node states) -> h_graph R^256, attention sums to 1. forward(target_genes, conditions, h_do) loops per-target subgraphs -> (h_graph (B,256), edge_gates dict-of-lists); targets absent from the PPI graph fall back to a zero h_graph. config: GRAPH_HOPS/NEIGHBORHOOD_CAP/GRAPH_HIDDEN_DIM/GRAPH_LAYERS/GRAPH_N_HEADS/EDGE_DROPOUT/EDGE_FEATURE_DIM/N_RELATION_TYPES/COMPLEX_EMBED_DIM/CONDITION_EMBED_DIM/RELATION_TYPES/PROTEIN_FEATURE_DIM. CPU and CUDA (device-aware; subgraphs moved to the module device). Verified: 8 synthetic tests in src/tests/test_graph.py (HeteroData structure, 2-hop cap, condition gate differs, signed-message tanh+relu, forward (4,256) finite, edge_gates per relation, zero-degree/absent target, readout attention sums to 1) + src/tcell_pipeline/graph/run_module2_smoke.py real-data smoke: builds the full 25440-node graph in ~18s, samples the CD3E neighbourhood (512 proteins / 740 complexes), runs real Module 1 h_do -> Module 2 h_graph (4,256) finite on GPU, gates differ across conditions, attention sums to 1. init.sh green (compileall + 46 pytest). UPDATE (2026-07-15, post code-review): fixed graph_builder degree column order to match Module 1 TARGET_SCALAR_KEYS [physical, functional, complex]; encode_one now moves h_do to the module device; edge-feature nan_to_num + gene-symbol dropna + unknown-PPI-source fail-fast; OOV culture_condition raises a legible ValueError (_condition_index, still fail-fast on the closed 3-value vocab); returned edge_gates[rel] is now length E (one per original edge) for all relations aligned to the sub-graph edge_index, not 2E-doubled for PP (full gate->edge identity-forwarding API deferred to Module 4); dead config N_RELATION_TYPES/RELATION_TYPES removed; flaky signed-message test seeded + false |out|<1 bound dropped; +2 regression tests.
